text: I wish they were a bit cheaper, but I love them. I have it in several colors & I can store a lot of yarn & several projects in each bag depending on the projects.  They also fit great in my cube storage system. I’m hoping that somebody will make one that fits perfectly into the 13” x 13” storage system I have at my home because I am already planning to buy more.  I’ve bought them in different colors so that I can remember which project is in which bag like color coding. It dorks for my old brain. No more digging through totes of yarn to find them. Lol.  As the bags are only about 8” deep I can currently keep all my knitting & crochet books behind the bags i my stutter cubes. It’s a win-win for me. I could probably get 2 in there side by side honestly, but so far I prefer them with 1 bag per cubbie.  The bags are great for other things too not just your stitching stuff.  I keep finding my dog Sophie’s toys on top of my knitting stuff. I’m going to have to buy her her own bag! Lol.  I love her, but I drew the line at pup saliva & pup hair all over my stitching & yarn.
Stamps great!

I bought a 2 kits for my daughter Sam, but her family kept doing them & she never got to. Text so I bought 6 more. This is the one my son-in-law Brandin picked to make.  They love that the kids ages 1-11 sit quietly & they all credit together. The irony is I bought these AFTER Xmas & it’s they really favorites. Best $ I ever spent.

Update: replace the order arrived today with this storage case more broken than the first one. Broken st bottom & at the closing snaps exactly where the first one was broken too. I give up. Time to order my art supplies elsewhere.

Old review: Seriously. Literally 6 of my recent orders have arrived with damaged or defective products. Some damaged/ defective during manufacturing & others possible damaged during packaging? Idk. I’m just sick & tired of repeatedly receiving items I purchase “new” that appear to be “seconds”, “returns”, or “lower quality” than expected. What really annoys me is in some cases there is no way in hell that the packager doesn’t see the obvious flaws when packing the products. The case on these pencils appears to have been damaged before shipping. I haven’t even tried the pencils yet bc the case is now useless for storage & needs replaced. Unreal.
The product I received had already been used!!!

Received what appears to be someone else’s return today. It’s disgusting. Idk if the ppl making it dropped in mud or poop or grease or what, but it’s covered in something black and nasty.  So I just ruined a ball of yarn trying this thing out bc it got on my yarn too.  That was how I noticed the issue. Been out of the box less than 10 minutes.  You can see it flakes off onto my white bed tray.  I have severe allergies. Not n owing what the substance is is making me a little crazy at the moment. I’m repulsed.

I do diamond painting and have been searching for a drafting type table top piece to try.  This works very well in my opinion. Especially helpful with larger projects. There are multiple adjustment points, 5 slots.  And most importantly there is a ledge piece that is about 1/2" deep.  Solidly holds my light board in place.  I use it on a folding table and the drafting table is stable. So far, so good.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

You are supposed to get 30 squares of wax and 10 cases.  I received 30 squares of wax and 9 containers. Set came in a sealed flatter box, so it was packed missing one container.  The cases are pretty cheap - they are supposed to screw closed - but the don't screw very easily because they are cheap plastic.  The wax squares came in a separate zip closed plastic bag and both sides of wax squares (3 rectangles, 2 rows of squares - 10 each) are both are covered, thankfully, in a clear plastic pages - which is good. The red wax is very sticky - because it's supposed to be. The squares and sheeting covering are pre cut, so you get one square easily.  So if you do diamond painting and you're looking for a better way to store your wax - this is worth a try - and you're ok not getting all the cases promised.

You get 2 kits, the teal green option and the purpley pink version.  I just completed the more teal version - the diamonds were fairly uniform in size and I did not run out... but I had VERY few spares - just FYI... don't spill any!  They are about 10"x12" finished.  I use a light board and have all the equipment you need to do these.  It does come with a tray, your stick pencil and some tweezers.  I would say that it is a good kit for the beginner - and depending on how much you work on it... done in a few days.  I would say a total of 8 -10 hours.  I am not a fan of the purple/pink version and will probably give it away.  When completed you can take them to a custom framer to be framed - can be expensive though.  So I always weigh and measure how much I like them when they are done.

Puts out a nice stream of hot air and works well.  Great for hobby use.  I tried a regular hair dryer and even on the lowest setting, the air stream who too strong. The cord is not very long, but about as long as on a soldering iron or curling iron.  These objects that heat up can't have long cords and probably not safe to run on an extension cord.

Have used Testors Plastic Cement for decades and it has always been my choice for assembling plastic models.  It is sticky enough to adhere well and dries well and makes a good bond.  It doesn't dry too fast, so you have a little time to move the parts around slightly to get a good fit, before you have a permanent bond.  There are glues out there that are more liquid, but was always afraid to use them for fear of them running too much and ruining the features on the plastic.  Note that this glue (and most plastic glues) works by dissolving a little of the plastic, so you do have to be careful not to use too much. If it oozes too much out of the joints you are gluing, it can damage the features of the plastic.  The only problem I have had with this glue is that you have to be careful to squeeze the tubes evenly or they can break through; they are made of a soft metal that tends to puncture if you aren't paying attention to how the tube gets crimped as you use up the glue.  Testors also makes a good line of paints for models.

This is a gorgeous, colorful, mostly green, John Deere themed train with nice detail.  Unfortunately it only has an engine, tender, one box car and a caboose so is not much of a train in the true sense.  I don't know what scale it is and train collectors like to know the scale when buying a train.  Of course this is for a young kid anyway, more so than for a collector or a little older kid who really is into trains.  It's fun for a short time, but just goes around in circles, so it's easy for a kid to lose interest quickly.  There are 32 pieces of track and several configurations you can do.  The radio control is a nice feature.  The only real downside I can see to this train is that it takes 6 "C" batteries to run and 3 "AAA" batteries for the remote.  That's a lot of batteries.  Make sure you have them on hand when you buy this in order to avoid disappointment by the child who receives this.  The batteries are NOT included.  It's a lot of batteries to replace too when they run low.  Maybe a re-chargable power pack would have been a better idea?  Was reading recently that toy trains are not as popular as they once were.  Before you spend on this, make sure your child really wants it.  It's a little expensive for what it is.  Probably better to spend your money on a "real" train set with at least 4 or 5 cars and expandable, maybe in HO gauge; something they can enjoy for more years than they could this set.  I always had the most fun building villages around the train when I was a kid, as even in a large train layout, the train still just goes around in circles.  This might be better suited as a just a display item, especially for someone into John Deere collectibles.  Or maybe something just to run around the bottom of a Christmas tree once a year.  Perhaps even as an eye-catching display in a store window.  It is not a toy that would be expected to bring hours of entertainment.  It is decent quality, but all plastic.  Made in China.

At first I really didn't like how oily they were stacked together. I literally sliced my thumb open on the first one I was trying to take off the pile. I sliced my thumb again as I was trying to wipe the oily residue off before loading the rotary cutter. So use and clean with a lot of care. I was on a time crunch to complete a project and didn't have the time for the accidents my first attempt caused me. 😂

However, as long as you are careful and use cautious care they were a great buy for a little price. I will purchase these again when I finish using all that came in the pack.

I did ask a quilter friend that regularly uses her cuter and wheels why they needed to be so gross with oil. I was told that it has to do with how many come in the stack and to keep them very sharp while being tossed about during the moving, handling, and shipping. I don't know if her answer is the reason or not but it did make sense to me.

Just handle with extreme care always. Do not let young children use a rotary cutter. And don't let teens that can barely cut with scissors use either. (I'm not being funny with the teen part they will hurt themselves.) Take my word for it. I had teens helping with the project. Only responsible people should use these extremely helpful tool! This was the best tool (along with the self healing mat) I was ever gifted!

I started this kit when I started graduate school in 2004, worked on it on again, off again over the years and finally finished it in September 2020… Even though I’ve been doing counted cross stitch for the last three decades, this is the largest project I have ever attempted and it looks spectacular, more like a tapestry or a painting. Boy did these paper soap sheets bring back some memories! I remember using them as a child, though I can't remember where or why. But as a kid, I thought they were pretty cool! These are definitely convenient to have. I will keep a package in my purse. It's frustrating to be at a public restroom somewhere and there's no soap! It's a little difficult to open the container, but the soap sheets are easy to use. They don't produce a lot of lather, but there's a little bit. It smells soapy. I like them! It also comes with some disposable towels. If I don't have access to paper towels, I end up shaking my hands dry, but this is a drier alternative. I'm actually going to put some of these sets in my kids (ages 19 & 22) stockings at Christmas!

My college kids and I recently started dabbling in the world of resin crafting. I wasn't really prepared the first time we crafted and it's a miracle that we didn't ruin the table! After that first night, I almost immediately ordered these. This is a set of 3 silicone mats. They are just the right size for our work. The mats clean up easily in addition to protecting our table. These could be used with all kinds of craft projects, not just resin.

Our 9 year old nephew spent a few days with us recently. This was a fun activity for him to do with my husband. It was easy to use. The instructions were clear. Our nephew was able to do this without much adult involvement. It deals with Science, Engineering, and possibly Mathematics as part of STEM learning. This would make a great gift!

I've been watching videos trying to teach myself fun lettering. These brush pens are just perfect for that. They're double ended with a larger brush tip on one end and a smaller one on the other. It has a nice variety of colors. They're adequately juicy.

A friend and I were recently trying some paper folding techniques to make ornaments. We had problems finding the best paper to use with it. Someone suggested origami paper and so I ordered some. My 21 year old son loves to draw. He does mostly pencil drawings. He prefers top wire pads such as this one. The paper is a good thickness and doesn't bleed through when using markers. It erases well and doesn't rip or tear when erasing. It's a good size for his drawing. These are comparable to sketch pads that I have purchased at the art supply store.

I've been making homemade soap for years. I have a wooden box that my husband made and it has to be held together with clamps. When you're finished, you just remove the clamps and the sides come apart. With that method, it was nearly impossible to get even stripes or designs in the actual soap. Plus, if the clamps weren't positioned correctly, liquid soap would leak out the sides. This mold is so much more reliable. You can layer your soaps to create some visual interest. I found that I do have to twist/move the edges a bit to get the finished hardened soap out, but it isn't too difficult. It's also super easy to clean up when you're finished. The cutting tools do a great job of cutting through the soap. I am very happy with this set.

When my kids were younger, I made slime with them. It was easy to make, but a pain to find Borax. I always had to go to several stores to try and find it. This kit is a huge time saver. Everything you need is in one kit. My nieces and nephew (ages 4, 7, & 9) were visiting recently and I thought this would be something fun for my 17 year old daughter to do with them. It didn't require a lot of supervision. They didn't have to scrounge around the house to find the supplies. It was easy to do and the results were perfect. All four of them had a lot of fun with it. In a time when kids have too much (toys), this would make a great birthday party gift!

My 20 year old son loves to draw in his free time. He goes through lots of drawing pads. He usually draws with pencils and he found this paper to work well with that medium. The paper is a good, mid-weight paper. He prefers spiral pads so he can flip all of the pages over when getting a blank one, but since this is bound at the top, it isn't as troublesome as a side binding pad would be. The paper is in between a bright white and a yellowish white. It is comparable to drawing pads we've purchased at the local craft store.

My teenage son needed a new sketch pad. He likes the size of this - it's larger than most of his other sketch pads. The spiral wires on the side and gets in the way a little bit, but he's getting used to it. I usually buy ones with the spiral wire on the top. The paper is perfect for his pencil drawings. Hopefully this will last him a few months. Time will tell!

My teenagers requested some art canvases to paint on this summer. I looked at the local craft store, but unless they are on sale, they are way too expensive. This set is just what I wanted and affordable. My kids have been using acrylic paints with these and they have worked fine.

My teenage daughter has been wanting to try to make bath bombs again. We tried a while ago and we weren't too successful. I decided to do more research this time around. And what I found was that metal bath bomb molds seem to work best. These molds  worked pretty well. I didn't think they were too light of material until I had to tap one half with a spoon. The spoon left small dents behind. For the most part, these worked very well. I think any problems that we ran into was due to the recipe adjustments, not the molds. It would have been perfect if there had been 3 halves instead of 2. The directions that I was following required the bath bomb to rest in one half for 10-12 minutes before removing the mold. So with these, we could only make 1 bath bomb every 10-12 minutes. If we had the third half, we could have done two at a time.

One of my co-workers "adopted" a few families for the holidays and I volunteered to get something for the 12 year old boy. On his list he had "drawing supplies". So many things I like about Sugru:
• takes no time at all to be ready for molding (no required kneading before it's soft enough to use)
• molds beautifully without sticking to you more than the object(s) you're using it on
• doesn't get dye everywhere while molding
• dries semi-soft but without give, similar to a good grip handle on hand tools
• amazingly repairs things I never thought could be repaired - like plastic drawers that crack down the side and center cracks to snap-on covers on plastic storage buckets
• holds up way better than anything out there like it!
• two or more colors can make new creative colors or stand out in swirl patterns and the like if you want something unusual
• can still be cut/scraped off if needed, leaving nothing behind (a soapy cleanser helps with final residue)

My ONLY complaint: I wish the packets came in larger sizes for the larger projects. Those little packets are each only enough for something like charge cord repair or a small hook. I'm often needing to use several packets together so it gets expensive very fast!

This yarn is so soft and easy to work with, I almost hate to even work with anything else! It's very smooth, drapes beautifully, and I have every expectation the ”anti-pilling” claim will hold true. The colorway is gorgeous too!

This DMHIRMG ZIPPER REPAIR KIT (2pcs; Black) includes a pincer tool for removing the slider stopper from a zipper, and a pair of needle-nose pliers for installing a new slider stopper. If you must replace a broken zipper slider, you will need both tools, or their equivalent. These are small specialty tools, with short handles.

The kit doesn’t include any instructions for use. However, there are lots of YouTube videos that show how to make zipper repairs. (The REI outdoor clothing outfitter has a particularly good video that uses tools like these--try Googling for “REI zipper repair”.)

The kit does NOT include any replacement parts (zipper sliders, stops, pulls, etc.). You can find these online. Also, Dmhirmg makes [[ASIN:B07K636SWN another zipper repair kit that contains an assortment of parts]].

This CRAFT-TASTIC TINY YARN TREE KIT makes a cute little thread-wrapped tree, IF you have the steady hands, the wrapping and knotting skills, and the patience to complete the project. The step-by-step instructions (with photos) are certainly clear enough, but the whole project is too fiddly for me.

Lining up different colors of embroidery floss, choosing attractive colors, and holding the threads taut as you carefully wrap them around the wire tree branches, is more difficult than it looks. Also, it’s up to the crafter to bend the wire tree trunk and branches—which arrives in a flat bundle—into a pleasing shape.

This is a nice kit that contains everything you need to make the thread-wrapped tree shown in the photo. It also looks like a decent kit to start with, if you want to try the thread-wrapping craft.

This SEEMAS LIGHTED HANDHELD 3-IN-1 MAGNIFIER SET delivers excellent value for the price (about $25 on Amazon). The 3X, 10X, and 16X lenses are easily interchanged for use with the lighted handle, and of course they can be used without the handle if you don't need the light.

I needed a lighted magnifier with really strong magnification to be able to clean wax from my husband's tiny, delicate hearing aids. The 16X lens, with the lighted handle, is perfect for the task. I had to learn to hold the glass really close to the hearing aid, though, to keep the lens from flipping the image upside down. (If you Google for "why do magnifying glasses flip images", you will find many easy-to-understand explanations of the science involved.)

The LED lights in the handle are very bright, and I love them. They are powered by three AAA batteries (not included). The lenses seem to magnify with little distortion. The lenses are made of acrylic resin, so they are less heavy than glass lenses, and not easy to break.

Teva Durham is an acquired taste: her designs are always innovative, but not always wearable enough or "pretty" enough that you would want to invest the time needed to make them. The designs in LOOP D-LOOP LACE are no exception: they represent the designer's latest experiments based on mesh stitches, eyelets, lace samplers, leaf stitches, and traditional lace doilies; and not all are successful.

Three designs that I like well enough to consider making (and which justify the book purchase) are the purse stitch cardigan with flower brooch (a simple, shaped, mesh cardigan), the closed bud pullover (a long-sleeved, high-necked sweater with a leaf-design yoke, upper body, and flared cuffs), and the hug-me-tight cardigan (a shrug constructed with an elaborate sideways-knit lace back panel, an openwork front panel, a picked-up collar, and picked-up elbow-length sleeves).

Three "wow" designs that should interest experienced knitters are the lace puff bloomers (pretty knee-length pants with lace at knees and waist only--but does anyone want to wear knitted bloomers today?), the art deco leaves cardigan (a long-sleeved allover-lace-pattern cardigan with flared sleeves and asymmetrical front closing), and the Shetland shawl dress (a hard-to-describe overdress with asymmetrical half-skirt, entirely constructed of diagonal panels of traditional Shetland lace patterns).

Among the less successful designs are the sunray medallion tunic (a flared-sleeve pullover with a huge doily medallion on the front), the split leaves ottoman cover (a large-scale-lace item knitted on US 15 needles at 12 stitches and 16 rows to 7 inches), and the various large-scale-lace shawls, cowls, boleros, and capelets knit in heavy yarn (the book cover photo shows a large-scale-lace bolero). The large-scale-lace designs provide near-instant knitter gratification, but, as a matter of personal taste, I always feel that heavy yarns overpower the whole concept of knitted lace. Shouldn't lace be delicate and complex?

As a book of wearable lace patterns, I rate LOOP D-LOOP LACE at 3 stars. As a book of Teva Durham designer patterns, I rate it at 5 stars, because the patterns move forward from the ones in Durham's previous book (see [[ASIN:1584794143 Loop-d-Loop: More Than 40 Novel Designs for Knitters]]); and because Durham has included many interesting explanations of her thinking that went into the creation of these new lace designs. Overall, this book merits 4 stars.

This is an electric-powered version of the familiar hand-cranked, clamp-on winder that knitters use to wind large skeins of yarn off a swift into center-pull balls. It is easy to use, portable (using the comfortable pull-up handle), attaches to any smooth surface with a strong suction cup (controlled by a hand-operated lever), and operates at variable speeds (giving the operator maximum control over the winding process). It produces a looser ball than a hand-cranked winder.

Unfortunately, the manufacturer did not see fit to include operating instructions in the box. Purchasers must scrutinize the photos on the outside of the box, or search the web for an instructional video, in order to understand how to thread and operate the winder. (The "official" how-to video can be found on the manufacturer's web site at Simplicity dot com under the tab for knitting accessories.)

This is a time- and labor-saving device for knitters like myself who frequently need to wind high-yardage skeins of yarn into balls. (The skeins must be divided into multiple balls, because the machine cannot wind a single huge ball.) However, it's a luxury knitting gadget, because it does not eliminate the need for a swift, and less expensive hand-cranked winders get the job done equally well.

If you have never knitted socks before, this knitting-bag-sized book (6"x 9") will tell you everything you need to know about knitting classic cuff-down socks. Instructions are included for three popular sock-knitting methods: (1) using double-pointed needles, (2) using two circular needles, and (3) using one long circular needle and the "Magic Loop" approach.

The book has 17 different sock patterns (some with extra variations), most of which call for slip-stitch heel flaps and simple stitch combinations. Nearly all of the patterns appeared previously in McCarthy's sock-shaped book, [[ASIN:1580175376 Knit Socks!]]. The patterns are presented in tables, and every step for each sock part (cuff, leg, heel flap, heel turn, gusset, foot, toe) is written out in detail. A great feature is that the tables give the stitch counts whenever the counts change (because of increasing or decreasing), including row-by-row counts when turning the heel. This step-by-step approach results at times in rather wordy instructions.

All of the patterns can be knitted by a novice sock knitter. "Winter Garden", the flowered cover design, is a more advanced, charted design that introduces the Fair Isle (stranded color knitting) technique. "Yoga Moves" includes an alphabet chart that can be used to personalize gift socks or add any short message around sock legs. "Off the Cuff" has an interesting fold-down cuff that uses the natural roll of stockinette stitch for an unusual cuff treatment. "Lacy Days" incorporates simple lace patterns; "Diamonds and Cables" and "Best Foot Forward" offer attractive cabled socks; and "You've Got Rhythm" presents three versions of textured-pattern socks.

There are patterns for many different weights of yarn, and at least one pattern ("Fireside Stripes") that is designed to show off handpainted yarn. Also included is a pattern with a stockinette heel ("Straight-Laced") and one with an "eye of peacock" heel ("Fireside Stripes").

If the competition among sock-knitting books weren't so fierce right now, this would be a 4- or 5-star book. However, because the patterns are so simple, the book is primarily of interest to new sock knitters. Therefore, the book rates only an "average" 3 stars.

Canvas is tight like it should be and is primed. Most companies claim that their canvas are primed, but they are not.  Some companies do not stretch the canvas correctly which makes them to loose to paint on. Very pleased with these.
I guess I didn't realize that these a very small spools.

This Acrylic Paint Set is very well done. It is packaged so nice! It is a perfect set for an Artist or aspiring one. It has 12 colors, 12 extra Brushes, and 6 small canvas waiting to become a future masterpieces! They are 4x6 size.  Perfect for a gift or for yourself
I love this small size sketch pad.  Perfect size for me.

There are eight colorful crepe paper streamers in this package. They are decent but not as wrinkly as other party streamers I've used previously. Nevertheless, these work well. At the current price of 9.99 ($11.99 minus $2), this comes to $1.25 per roll.

Besides this set, I have the Jo.arstom Colored Pencils (Amazon Item # B08CZFB6Z3) which I ordered for $11.98 and the LYRA Rembrandt Aquarell Artists' Colored Pencils, Set of 72 Pencils  (Amazon item # B0012EXEA6) which I paid $104.51 for and now sells for $75.27. After coloring with all these pencils, I feel this set is excellent at the current price of $24.99.

There are 72 sharpened colored pencils in a canvas wrapped case. Also included is a low-end pencil sharpener, which works surprisingly well, and a pencil extender where only the narrower side fits the pencils in this set. What I love the most, besides the "case," is that the pencil colors are not waxy and crayon-like. Instead they are more like my LYRA set where the pigment is matte. 

I used about 17 of the 72 pencils to color the image pictured. I did use 3 colors from my LYRA set and they are noted by the three POST-IT flags that are sticking up in the photo. The difference between this set and my LYRA pencils is almost imperceptible. The LYRA Rembrandt colors tend to be more subdued but realistic.

In summary, these sharpen well and color well. The pigment is more vibrant than my expensive LYRA Rembrandt pencils and much less crayon-like than the low-end Jo.arstrom set. These are excellent for the price and perfect for the child or adult who enjoys coloring with a coloring book. For those who want to frame their works, want a more realistic and softer image, or a matte finish and are more than just a part-time artist, I recommend the high-end brands such as LYRA but they cost substantially more than this. For the current price of $24.99, I feel this set is a good buy.

These gift bags are sturdy. Since they have no design, the bag acts like a blank canvas for an artist to create their own message, drawing, or design on the bag (if desired). However, they are very small. It fits a box of Bigelow 100ct tea bags comfortably with only about two inches to spare to the left or right of that box as shown in my photo. Anything of equivalent size will fit in this.

The set supposedly comes with 25 bags, 100 tags and tissue paper, and string. However, I received 27 bags, 94 tags, one long piece of twice, and what looks like less than 100 sheets of tissue paper. I got so tired of counting the tags and bags that I refused to count the tissue paper but it definitely does not look like 100 sheets.

I am not sure why so many tags are included. Since there are only 25 bags, only 25 tags should be needed. The string must be cut to fit and I doubt there will be enough string for 100 tags but there is definitely enough for 25. In addition the tissue paper provided is sufficient for 25 bags as it does appear I received about 50 sheets of those.

In summary, these bags are well-made. The quantity listed on the site is not the quantity of everything received (at least not what I received). The current price of $29.99 comes to $1.20 per gift bag set if 25 bags, etc. is received. This price is competitive and a bit more reasonable than some other bags I have reviewed on the market. If a small, sturdy bag is needed for gifting and one wants the potential to decorate it, then this fits the bill.

There are a total of eight sheets of dots (each sheet has 50 dots) in this package. There are four sheets with the female side of the dot and four sheets with the male side of the Velcro dots. They are highly adhesive and stick well. I used this to stick up an edge on a poster I have (see photo) that kept coming loose from my bathroom door.  I love the adhesion. I have no idea how easily this will be to remove--lucky I am not interested in removing it. The current price of $6.99 is reasonable for this.

I ordered this for household projects and not macrame; although, this cord is of a good width for that. There is an instruction booklet (see photos 2-5) on how to tie macrame knots. I like that it does come with this booklet but am not likely to use it.

Nevertheless, I am using this cord on household projects. It is about 1/8" thick so it is great for tying down recycling boxes, newspapers, larger plant stems to railings, hanging planters and hummingbird feeders, etc. I used it to tie down some packaging material and it worked very well. After cutting the cord to length, the ends do come apart. Frayed ends can be difficult to work with but that is to be expected when using any cord of this width.

Overall, this is good, strong cord. I have no idea what this will be priced at. Based on my research it seems a price between $14 and $17 seems to be the going rate for 220 yards of cord in this size.

I am very impressed with the capacity of this magnifying set. It comes with glasses whose ear rims can be switched out for the supplied elastic band. There is a light on top to illuminate the area of focus. Each of the five lenses easily slips in and out of the front of the glasses.

WHAT'S IN THE BOX

- Eyeglass frame with light

- Stretch headband to switch out ear rims, if desired

- Cleaning cloth

- Case with 5 magnifying lenses (1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.5)

There is no 3.0 magnifying lens which surprises me. Also, this cannot be used with glasses. I had to remove my glasses to use this eyewear. The nose guards did bother me a bit, so this is not something I can wear for long. However, for quick precision work like removing a watch battery, threading a needle, or reading some small print, then this is perfect. The current price of $11.98 is also excellent for what you get. Currently, it is even cheaper with a 5% off coupon.

I purchased one package at Buddy's Small Lots for $1.29 plus tax thinking that this was worth a try. I tried this on two broken zippers (a jacket and blanket wrap). They did not work at all.

First, the plastic piece on the zipper head broke when taking it apart as instructed making it useless and no longer able to close. In fact, there was no way to open the zipper head without breaking the plastic. Once broken, it would not stay closed.

Second, it does not work on zippers that completely separate apart, like on jackets and blanket wraps, because the solid piece on those zippers is not removable and only stands in the way of this zipper head.

This product is absolutely useless based on my results. I even had my boyfriend try it and he was laughing at how this does not work at all and breaks every time. I just wasted my money.

was an excellent addition to the wifes charm bracelet. As we own 2 miniature schnauzers this sterling silver charm was ideal

These jump rings are excellent quality and have a nice bright silver plated finish. They are all open exactly as shown and described! They save time and effort connecting lobster claw clasps and charms! They look beautiful and close and align with a narrow nose pliers. I highly recommend and would purchase again when ever needed.

These lobster claw clasps are pretty and a convenient size able to hold up to a maximum of 3 jump rings with small attached charms, but when initially opened, several have immediately fallen apart, with the small thin middle bar piece falling out. They are questionably fragile, so I handle them very gently and carefully. If you don't mind losing about 20% of the 200 that are not strong and secure, the rest are reliable and do look really beautiful. If you buy these make sure you test the clasp before attaching any jump rings to it, so if it instantly breaks you won't have to start over from scratch. I used 6mm size jump rings with these clasps, and they worked out great. You could use 5mm. I make it as easy as possible.
I would recommend adding an extra amount to cover the amount of breakage. The value may already reflect that, but I would make sure if you want repeat customers. Maybe I got a bad batch?
They served my purpose, and the bright sterling silver plate looks consistently flawlwss and beautiful.
Great for a gift. My husband was very happy

Small but exactly what I needed for cutting yarn and thread. They are quite sharp with pointed tips for precision cuts. I wouldn't use them on fabric unless it was to cut small v's or something that needed just tiny cuts. They are well made. But if you have large fingers they may not be comfortable to use.

Perfecto for the candle making enthusiasts.  Quality is decent, and the wicks allow a low burn, which is what I prefer with my selection of glass jars. If you have a super wide ja then I recommend using 2 wicks otherwise you will be left with a lot of wax clinging to the sides: then you mealtime it and remake more candles!

The tips are super thick, which was hard to manage at first.  That said, the ink flowed very well and just a tad too much.  I used it with any magnetic writing surface from the chalk paper to white plastic. It cleans off easily with wet cloth.

This is great for clothings and in my case, cushions.  It is so thin that you can’t tell or feel it  Very easy to sew into cotton.  Adhesion is great as I wasn’t ‘unzipping’ daily, only when the cushion covers need cleaning.  I m so glad I found this as I was struggling to find the right closure for the cushions.

Love these pens for more than just art.  The quality is top and they don’t dry out quickly.  We still using them after several months.  The colors are beautiful and the range gives you enough to do casual and yet detailed art.  Very comfortable to hold.  This brand has become a family favorite.

My first smudge feather, perfect size, and arrived in great shape (mars bazaar). You have to be careful with all feathers as it will split open, which makes smudging a little ineffective. Great little handle for ease of use.  I have it perched on my huge pearl shell! So happy with it.

I was hugely surprised on the excellent quality of these needles, solid, smooth and as good as the Clover takumi brand.  For the price, these needles are excellent value for beginners or avid knitters.  Love the bamboo needles, green and light to handle: love them.

This case is really best for colored pencils. It will also hold Stabilo pens perfectly. But chunkier markers and things, well, they don't fit as well. You can put them in, you just have to skip every other slot, so it doesn't hold as many. The "pages" come out since they are velcroed in place.
This is a nice set of wooden ornaments. There is a decent variety. I’m not sure why the markers— markers on wood is always a poor choice. They work much better with acrylic paint, glitter glue, etc.

These leave a lot to be desired. They aren't very pigmented. They don't go on very smoothly. The tips are just odd. I'd definitely stick with Tombow or Micron pens over these. These aren't even close in quality and performance.

My kids have become flipbook junkies. They love making flipbooks. These are really easy to put together and the metal brads hold really well. If you're just starting out, you'll need a lightbox to make flipbooks. This set is just extra paper, covers, and brads. They're good quality. The packaging also kept the components separate from each other, which I thought was pretty clever.
This binder is really big. It holds lots of card pages. You can add more pages if you want. They’re just standard 3-hole binder card pages. It also zips, so no worries about losing cards.

I bought these scissors SPECIFICALLY to make pompoms, and they did not disappoint! They are not only cute, and in my favorite colors, but are small enough to slip in my yarn bag. And the protective sleeve is a must, given how sharp they are. I highly recommend them! As someone who uses yarn daily, I needed these so much! And I also want to point out the protective sleeve has a hole in the tip, in case of accidental swallowing.

These are great if you don't want to carry around scissors. They cut yarn fairly well, but not super precise. Every time I have used it, I always end up having to trim the tail of yarn a little. I'm super OCD about my  yarn ends, since I make hats and other items to donate to charity. If you don't mind a little fuzzy yarn end, then it's fine. I love that I can easily put a piece of yarn through the loop and attach it next to my needle holder and stitch counter; both of which I also got on Amazon. So much better than having to worry about losing your scissors while your at the Dr's, etc. It's also very pretty, and just looks like a medallion. And with a set of two, in two different colors, you can keep one and share with a friend! Very good value, as well! I would definitely recommend this to any serious crafters!

It came quickly,  and well-wrapped. My only issue, and the ONLY reason I ordered this yarn in the first place, is to use to make Seahawk hats and scarves. I compared my last batch of my Spring Sparkle (from Walmart- same brand/color) to this new batch, and the new batch is much darker. It looks more olive than spring. I even asked a Seahawks fan if the new was too dark for Seahawks, and she said 'Definitely! Send it back!' So I can't use any of this, which delays my orders for my customers.

This tape might be best decoratively rather than functionally to hold something together.  The tape is very thin and kind of fragile, but absolutly beautiful.  We have used it as highlights in cards and to decorate a small art project, but have had less than stellar results when trying to use it as tape.  So we will use it for what it is.  Elegant and beautiful decorative tape.

I really wish this was a lot thicker and more presentable for a portfolio.  It is a good organizer for art, but because it is a very thin plastic, it does not make a great presentation portfolio.  It does fill up a bit when you fill the plastic sleeves, but not something you can use for interviewing.  I love the tabloid size and I will probably use it to store larger vinyl cut prints in when I work in this size. Good idea, just wish they had beefed it up a bit.

I had been looking for gears for a while and could only find highly overpriced packages with a half dozen gears until I discovered this line of gears.  I have a different bigger assortment in brass and that one seems a little more solid than these blackened steel version.  But these have the same great look and work perfectly into my designs.  My oldest has claimed some to put into his warhammer models.  So glad I found these, there are a lot of gears for $10 to $12.  Flip through the similar items to find other great packages.

This is a decent little collection and augments my current clay tool collection fairly well.  The quality is ok and feel like they will work for quite a while.  We have been taking clay classes at a local community center and have started doing a lot of sculptural work and these are all nice and small, perhaps too small.  You will definitely want some bigger tools as well and perhaps a longer needle point, but for me it definitely fills some holes.  Price is decent, far better than my local art supply stores.  All in all, a decent little collection.

I really like the sense of control this pen gives. I tend to do a lot of tight art work with fine markers and pencil marks and this Glue Pen gives me that kind of control. I never felt like I had that kind of control with the pistol grip guns. It did take a few shots to get the feed rate correct but now I feel that I can achieve and accuracy of application I was never able to approach with the old style. I do have to point out that I have done some air brushing so the controls are somewhat familiar but I think they are intuitive enough for anyone to pick up.

I love that this uses the mini sticks that we have in abundance and love the whistles and bells like the color changing tip that lets you know when the tip is hot. I also appreciate how quickly it is ready to use. Again, I feel that I have better control by knowing when it is ready to go and when it is ready to be stored. The spring loading is interesting and loads and feeds well.

This is a huge step up in technology for glue gunning. It is reasonably priced and well worth the investment if you use glue guns even occasionally.

These types of art kits have always been a big hit with the kids and this one is no different. And most of these kits produce kind of useless, hard to display pieces of art so that the value is in the process, and part of the price is finding a way to get rid of the end product without hurt feelings. I really thought this one would go this way, but I really like how this ended up as a useful necklace hanger in the end. It is a little eclectically coloroed, but it sits on the desktop well and it looks nice with the jewelry hanging from it.

As for value, the kids tore into it and spent over an hour twisting the yarn around the branches. It was finished within a few hours of arriving, but in dollars per entertainment value it wasn't bad.

This kind of product is useful for attaching things together when tape isn't workable or when using nails/screws would cause damage. I looked to this stuff to help me mount a paper towel holder under a cabinet. It did not work out for me as well as I had hoped.

This is basically wide strips of Velcro with sticky adhesive on the back. It's wider than other Velcro I've used, and in some cases this would be mildly problematic. That said, it is easy enough to cut to fit the need, so I'd rather have it wide like this and trim it than to have something that is too narrow and needs to be augmented.

The issue with this stuff is that the adhesive sticks better to the surface on which it is attached than to the Velcro, and as such the adhesive tends to separate from the Velcro backing. Losing a paper towel holder with a roll on it isn't a particularly big deal, but if it is relied upon to attach something more fragile or more valuable it could be a big problem.

In my case, double-sided tape works better. Others may have more success, but this is a product I will not be using. Hopefully I just got some from a bad batch.

I am very much a rank beginner when it comes to art and artwork but I have been learning to draw and I am always looking for new ways to improve my skills. Even as a child I enjoyed the process or tracing images, so this device is very much a revisit to the past for me, though I never had anything so sophisticated. This is a straightforward and simple device and it works well.

This is basically an illuminated platform for tracing and to a lesser extent drawing. It runs on USB power though it comes with a plug for connecting to AC if desired, and it does not require special tools or extra equipment.

The top surface on which you work is illuminated and is very bright – bright enough to light up a room sufficiently for finding your way through at night (though there are better things for this purpose). The idea is simple – place the drawing you wish to trace on the surface, then place a sheet of paper on top and trace it. It is very simple and easy. Now that said, adding color and shading and handling some of the more subtle elements of drawing does require a little more patience and skill, but this is a good way to develop those skills so you can draw your own when you are creating your own artwork.

The are two modes with this device – on and off. When it is on the light level adjustment can be made by holding down the power button. The power button is a touch control and it is very sensitive – it works at even a soft touch.

One thing that I would have liked to have seen with this device is some kind of clip to hold your work still while you are working. For me, it has a tendency to slip a little bit and I find myself adjusting the position periodically. A small, snug but not tight rubber clip is all that would be needed to keep things still without damaging the whole. This is not a deal breaker by any stretch of the imagination and a small piece of clear tape is sufficient to keep things in place too.

Although there is a sheet in the box with some warnings included, it is worth repeating – do not use really hard, sharp objects with this device which will damage the surface. The surface is acrylic and is susceptible to scratches, so use some care. Cleaning the surface if necessary is not hard and can be done with a dry cleaning cloth – preferably microfiber.

This is a very nice and very functional tracing tool that does a good job of brightly backlighting things you wish to trace. This is great for duplication of illustrations and artwork (though it is going to be very tedious on very detailed illustrations) and it is a great tool for someone learning to draw to develop technique. It is not cost prohibitive either which is a big plus.

Disclosure: I was provided a sample of this product in exchange for a fair, honest and thorough review. Even though I received this product at minimal or no cost I have rated and reviewed this product without bias, based solely on my experience with it.

A jeweler’s loupe is a useful tool not just for jewelers but also for coin collectors. With two avid coin collectors in the house a loupe gets used frequently for examining just about every coin that passes through our pockets. I was given the opportunity to evaluate this loupe and so far I have been very, very pleased with it.

This is a two-lens loupe that has two different magnification powers. The larger lens is a 30x magnification lens, the smaller 60x. Choose the detail level you desire and that determines the lens, and thus the magnification as well. Both lenses have their uses in the context of coin collecting. The larger lens is good for checking out major detail lines and scratch marks while the smaller is for looking at finer lines and discerning more faded details.

Both lenses have their own LED light but both lights cannot be on simultaneously. The light is sufficient to really illuminate the surface at which it is facing, but a little care is needed to make sure that the LED is not covered while holding the loupe. The LEDs are quite small so it is easy to cover them up inadvertently if being inattentive.

I did a little experimentation with both lenses to see at what point everything was completely out of focus. The smaller was good to about a quarter of an inch, consistent with the 12mm advertised focal length and the larger was focused to about one inch, which is close to the 22mm advertised focal length. Distortion is minimal around the edge of the lens even as is passes out of focus.

This loupe comes in an egg-shaped clamshell case that provides very good protection. The case is nothing especially fancy but it is sufficient to protect the loupe from most falls and crush damage but it is not impervious to damage from sufficient impacts. In short, as long as it is not abused the case should be fine protection for this loupe.

Finally, I want to mention that this is not an expensive item at all, currently selling for less than $10 as I write this (price can change so keep this in mind).

Overall we are happy with this loupe and are pleased to recommend it. It works great, it is inexpensive and the case in which it ships provides good protection. It is great for viewing very small details in things like coins and jewelry.

The FixNZip is one of the most amazing products I've ever used. A few months ago I purchased an amazing bag. Unfortunately, whoever made it didn't tack down the seam next to the zipper, so it kept getting stuck and ruined the zipper head. I Love this bag and it's no longer sold anywhere. I was trying to find a tailor to replace the zipper on my bag, and I came across the FixNZip. My zipper is closed on both ends so you can't just slide a new zipper head on the bottom. I watched a short FixNZip video that showed how to install it, and that it would work on a closed system zipper. Then I ordered it. When it arrived, it took less then 2 minutes to install it. It works great! The reason the old zipper broke is because they didn't tack down the material by the zipper. Now when the material gets caught in the zipper, all I have to do is turn the screw a tiny bit and pull the fabric out. FixNZip is amazing!
So easy to use! Love how it turned out. New obsession

These stencils have a great texture and are easy to clean, but I have mainly used mine for adding spray-painted alphabetic and numerical accents onto my mixed media canvas pieces, so messy overspray hasn’t concerned me much. I do like that they’re flexible (but sturdy) and semi-opaque so that I can easily judge proper placement by verifying the locations of other number and letter edges around whichever stencil I have placed, which is an attribute sign makers will really appreciate. Each stencil does arrive with a punched out hole present in the corner, so customers can easily use the provided silver ring for organized containment of this set, but I cannot for the life of me understand why the number “8” is cut out in a different font from all of the other numbers? If it weren’t for that one obvious inconsistency, this product would have easily earned 5 stars. Despite that one outlying discrepancy, I’m otherwise pleased with their performance!

This 6-roll set of decorative washi tape is undeniably pretty! The girly bow pattern offers a whimsical aesthetic, and the pastel shades feel reminiscent of Easter! Since 3 of the rolls are wider than the other 3, you do receive some size variety, and the set also includes a flexible, clear plastic grid accessory. The adhesive qualities seem to be reliable, and the decorative pattern is perfect for journals and cards. The metallic silver accents on every roll exhibit a holographic, rainbow sort of iridescence in the shifting light, and that is definitely my favorite attribute about the set as a whole!

The rainbow iridescence on this stainless steel pair of scissors is beyond magical! I do love a whimsical craft supply, and these little birds are both cute AND reliable! The scissored hinge construction seems smooth, and the ergonomic design makes each of them incredibly comfortable to hold and manipulate, especially when needing to make close cuts at odd angles. I’ll be embroidering up a storm with these feathered flights of sharp-edged fancy! These would make an excellent (and memorable) gift for any friend or family member who loves to craft with flair.

This complete set of gouache paint colors offers a wide range of creative possibilities! I’ve only opened specific colors as I need them, but for the little tubs that have been exposed to air, only the edges seem to need further stirring and re-mixing when I open the case to use them another time. I like that this set came packaged with some brushes, although it’s impossible to keep them white, even with immediate washing after each use, so buyers should know these paintbrushes do stain easily. The lid to the case snaps on tightly, and I’ve had no issues with secure closure. Once used, each shade of paint dries very quickly (with the expected matte finish), and I’ve seen no issues with eventual cracking or color-loss issues. I think this is a great set, affordably priced, and nicely packaged. I’d recommend it to other artists!

Love the colored assortment of this quality set of pastel paper sheets. The thickness is ideal (offering both flexibility and an upscale look) and the sheets also provide a nice base/background for some of my mixed media collage assemblages!

As a mixed media artist, I’m always open to trying new products and consistencies, textures, colors, mixtures, and intuitively abstract paint formulations! I do love the generous size of these tubes, and the colors mix smoothly together. The thickness seems to vary a little between hues, so if you are using them in a warm environment, they can get a little runny. I highly recommend unscrewing the lids with the tubes pointing UP or gravity can coax some of the colors to drip out immediately upon opening. I also encountered some notable transparency issues when painting on canvas, so it required an application of multiple coats to (eventually) achieve full coverage. All in all, this paint set is great for little amateurs!

✔️Easy to squeeze and dispense
✔️Clearly labeled, wide range of colors
✔️Stray smudges and mishaps rinse off easily
✔️The neon and glitter options are whimsical ✔️IDEAL kids’ art product! 🎨

As an entrepreneurial artist 👩🏼‍🎨 with mixed media experience and online sales, I was anxious to assess the quality of this acrylic paint marker set! I can say that I greatly enjoyed having the two-tip option/duality, since it makes broad coverage and the creation of straight lines easily attainable. The paint is definitely opaque, but each layer dries super quickly, and a couple of layers provide full and complete coverage. With enough coats, you can even layer a lighter shade on top of a darker one with no show-through. Once dry, the paint is, indeed, absolutely waterproof. The flow remains more even with the broad tips, as the fine points occasionally collect an excess paint drip at the tip. But if you stay mindful of the flow and shake well as suggested, they are quite reliable!

I decided to use these pens to freehand paint/draw on a couple of gallery-wrapped mini canvases. I think this pair of colorful 🐦 birds with color-blocked backgrounds turned out quite well! The testing process will be fully complete once I use the included mandala stencils to add some adhesive for loose glitter or silver leaf application.

My only potential negative involves a discrepancy between what appears on Amazon and what was delivered. As shown in one of the attached photos, the set of 24 colors that I received did not include a dozen metallic options as mentioned in the product listing and title. My labeled box of 24 contained: (12 Classic colors) as expected, and (12 “Natural“ colors). Since the classic set already comes with a gold and a silver option, I did still at least get to sample their metallic tendencies.

Overall, I am quite pleased with the quality and versatility of this acrylic paint pen set. Even seasoned artists can add this product to their paint supply arsenal; I’d gladly recommend them!

🎨 Once I got this easel ready for action, I found it to be sturdy, lightweight, and easily adjustable. It can support a variety of wrapped canvases, and it is easy to store flat against a wall or transport outside the home for presentations or festival displays!

My greatest issue involved the provided assembly instructions. They are not inherently straightforward. The one-page sheet has no words, only a numbered diagram. It takes a bit of spatial imagination to grasp a clear comprehension of which piece is which, and how they might possibly fit together. Once that puzzle becomes clear, the entire easel can be easily assembled in 15 minutes with the included Allen wrench.

In my delivered package: the super thin, plastic bag containing the metal hardware bits had apparently ripped open at some point during storage or shipment, and assorted screws and wing nuts and washers were tumbling loose throughout the entire length of the box. Thankfully the cardboard itself was well-sealed enough without gaps to prevent any escaped littles. All pieces were present and accounted for. Overall, I think this is a valuable and versatile art supply piece that makes painting assistance a breeze! 🔆

Purchased this pearlescent PanPastel stack after finding such enjoyment with a previous order of the basic colors set. They apply easily with the included applicators, and they blend silky smooth onto sanded art paper (you can even use your fingers to further blend, smudge, spread, and blur section edges together)!

My only issue is that the top “Blue” color in the stack arrived broken, so twisting the cap off exposed tons of powdery crumbles - like what would result from a dropped palette of eyeshadow. So perhaps a firmer binding technique for the pearlescent shades could be incorporated into the formulaic ingredients somehow. Otherwise, quite happy with them!

I love these gel pens! I tested each one by signing my name and I only had issues with one pen skipping (it was one of the standard blue shades.) This set has so many shades of each color, especially green. Going by the caps it looks like there are a lot of black/grey pens, but a lot of them are actually shades of brown. There are even a few white/cream colored pens with and without shimmer. These pens are perfect for coloring in those super-detailed adult coloring books that I hated coloring before because I was always sharpening colored pencils. I've even used them with colored pencils and markers on the same piece with fabulous results. Keep in mind that gel pens put out wet ink that can be moved to lighten or darken an area before it dries. If you run the pen tip through wet ink with full contact on the paper you will "streak" that area. It can be fixed after it dried with another coat of ink. I feel the review with the color swatch photos is misleading and because of this I tested the colors with cursive (one continuous stroke in several different directions.) I did not have the streaking issue that the other reviewer had. Also, it should be noted that none of my pens were busted upon arrival. The case is awkwardly large and not fun to use but the price was great so I'm not complaining.
I've included photos of my color test sheet (blurred) and a few post cards I've colored with these pens, with and without flash to show the glitter.

When I came across the skyyue DIY 5D Diamond painting kit, I almost scrolled past it without stopping to take a look. I SHOULD have scrolled past it without taking a look. But I stopped to see what it was about. When I saw the part about it being a stress reducer in the info section, I decided to try it. Our family has been going through a horrific situation for the past 2.5 years that has taken a toll on us all.  I figured if this was something that could help my stress, I wanted and needed to try it. So I placed my order. I love the design-flamingos.  Beautiful, colorful birds. I was  looking forward to this  art project.  As you can see in my photos, the canvas itself arrived rolled up. You can see the indentations throughout.  It doesn't lay flat, it pops up and rolls up/curls at the ends.  Even keeping it flat with books on top didn't help. Its also frayed in areas.  There are next to no instructions. There's a few at the bottom of the canvas which really didn't  help much and the writing is unbelievably small, even when I wear my prescription reading glasses.  "Insert the round end of the dotting pen into the dotting cement". CEMENT?  I have NO clue where to find cement. NO cement of any kind was included.  Is the  cement to be purchased separately?  I  do not know.  The "diamonds" are nothing like diamonds. Obviously, I knew they weren't going to be real.  But these are little plastic beads. Thousands of  beads  in tiny plastic bags.  One bag was torn open with beads spilled out everywhere.  In order to do this project, you're going to have to have a small container for  each of the various colored beads' storage. Perhaps a dixie cup (though you'll want a lid) or one of those tiny  plastic condiment containers you can get at the dollar store. (FYI...store carefully around kids/pets...these beads are TINY) So much for my 'stress free' activity. In light of the fact that the canvas arrived rolled up and frayed, no clue where to find cement, no decent instructions, and spilled beads, there's no reason to even attempt to start this kit. Even if I figure out the cement situation, I don't think the "diamonds" will stay in place in all of the areas where the canvas is bent.  If It were to complete this, or complete what I could of it, why on earth would I frame and hang a massively bent canvas?

Tulip hooks are my favorite out of all of the hooks I own, or have tried. (I have not tried every brand available.). This package of Wrapping Tissue Paper & Kraft Tags & Bags, EUSOAR Kraft Packing 3in1 Package comes with everything you need to wrap 25 gifts or party favors. I got these mainly so so I can cheat next Christmas but also for random birthdays throughout the coming year.

I chose the 15.7"x5.9"x11.8" option and the bags I got are indeed 15.7"x5.9"x11.8". I got 25 bags, 100 sheets of paper and 100 tags. The bags are thick and sturdy.. very nice quality! I've got some metallic pens that I cannot wait to use on these bags. The perfect way to personalize your gifts to the recipient! The bags also have very nice handles that seem like they'll stand up to a decent amount of weight and reuse. The paper is better than dollar store paper and I love that you get 100 sheets. Since these bags are so large, you'll need more than 1 or 2 sheets per bag.. with this package, I got 100 sheets which works out to 4 sheets per bag. The tags are made well too! They're decently sized, not too small, and are thick and come with twine to tie them to the handles of the bag.

Bottom line.. this is a very nice, high quality set of gift wrapping! When I run out, I'll most certainly be ordering more. I definitely recommend it!

This cotton yarn is decent quality but I wouldn't use it on anything that needs to be soft.  I tried a few different crochet stitches and patterns, but its just not soft. These hoops are plenty sturdy, but I was a bit disappointed that they were not all perfectly round.  There were some spaces even when tightening them down with thick fabric.  I wouldn't mind so much for wooden hoops, but these are plastic so there is no excuse for gaps in the hoops.

This leather cord is exactly as pictured.  The smell is great and the cord will be wonderful for necklaces or detailing leather work.  This is probably not intended to support a lot of tension, so try to have realistic expectations.  I wouldn’t use these for shoelaces or other applications which require more strength.  It’s beautiful cord, just test the weight before you begin your project.

These ocean blue glass beads are beautiful.  Each bead is rough and unique.  These are shipped already strung on a fiber based cord, which is a great example of how they could be used.  They look like round sea glass, and slightly frosted.  I highly recommend these beads if you are making either ethnic jewelry or western jewelry.

All three of the boxes arrived completely hardened out.  The clay inside the boxes was not wrapped in any plastic, so perhaps the individual boxes themselves have drawn out the oils from the clay.  I attempted to chip off a small piece of the clay from a corner of the block, and it was very brittle and had no malleability.  I trust the Sculpey brand, as so many of the sculptors that I watch on Youtube use it.  Maybe mine was just a bad batch?  Maybe it was just really old?  Either way, the clay needs to be checked before it is shipped out. (And no, this was not salvageable with baby oil or any other substance.  It would have been like mixing completely dry flakes of clay with oil.  I think that would take hours, which is not the intent of this product and not worth the price if I have to condition it myself with my own oils.)

This cutter glides very smoothly and because of the larger wheel you can easily make sure that you are making straight cuts.  This cutter glides through multiple material layers like butter.  I'm anxious to see the longevity of the blade since I do a lot of cutting and I was buying numerous replacement blades for my older rotary.

I really like this little row counter that I use frequently when I crochet. I drew a length of ribbon through the key hole and wear it around my neck so I don't lose it and remember to graduate each row as I crochet to keep in pattern.  Highly recommend for the quality and the very low price

As a quilter who makes tons of pieced squares and 1/2 square triangles for pieced quilts, this is an invaluable aid to getting my piecing straight. I've tried the fade away markers, but in completing fast triangle they fade away too quiickly and I can't see the lines as they fade out. This is permanent, but it ends up on the cutting edge so it isn't a problem. Highly recommend!

I needed these tri beads for a project for my second graders. Unfortunately the beads are so small that the kids had problems manipulating them onto the pipe cleaners which took a 15 minute art project into a 1/2 hour art project.  Additionally, the packages appear much 'fuller' or fatter on line than the actual product is, requiring me to purchase additional beads from a different supplier. I was disappointed because of these two issues, but if older kids will be using them and you don't need as many to complete the project, they may fit the bill.

I absolutely love my new sewing table!  The drop leaf extends very far, fits the area I wanted it in perfectly, very roomy to spread out my quilt projects and with the cabinet on the right, very handy for extra fabric and other notions. I love the fact that my sewing machine fits perfectly under the closed door when not in use.  My only complaint could be the color, advertised as Cherry, but the wood is VERY  dark brown - coffee colored with limited reddish tones prevalent in most other cherry furniture.  Other than that, and it also took me about 4 hours to assemble it, by my self, but the instructions were very clear and I had no trouble getting the pieces to fit where indicated.  The Sewing station was very well packaged, box-in-box lined with styrofoam and all of the pieces arrived unscratched/undamaged. One caviat, the inside plastic notion storage pockets were packaged inside one another and stuck terribly.  I had to soak them in hot soapy water for over an hour to get them apart from one another.  The table height is perfect for sewing so no more back aches!  I love this piece and would buy another if my daughter decides to learn to sew.

Nice product, comes conveniently packaged on a spool as shown. The problem with this was the long delay in arrival, taking in excess of six weeks.  I've purchased locally for just a dollar or two more - not worth waiting when you have a project in mid-completion.

These are not for use on boats or any marine environment. The description needs to be changed. It is misleading & people who don't know any better will use these & they will rust. If you don't need them to be SS then maybe they are ok, but I didn't use any. Sent them back immediately.
