People who like to craft need certain supplies in abundance. If sewing and creating things to wear or for around the house is of interest, then you need fabric and since I live with a young crafter, I decided to add this acrylic felt fabric to her stockpile.

Measuring about 72 inches by 18 inches, this fabric is soft and ready to cut any way you need it. The thickness is sufficient to use it for anything that requires something a little sturdier, but it’s not so thick that it’s difficult to sew. It is soft and smooth to the touch and can be used for many things.

There is a nice assortment of colors with this felt. The one I got is black, but you can select among several. It’s useful for many craft projects and last I checked, I think my teenager was going to use it to create pillows. It has lots of uses and is certainly a good choice for many crafting needs.

When you craft, you have lots of supplies and you need a way to store these supplies in some way. If you sew, then you likely have dozens of spools and a good, efficient way to store and access with ease is this Wooden Thread Rack from JiocoVino.

You will need to assemble this rack, but it comes with the screws you need and even a small screwdriver. I found the screwdriver a little too small and substituted a larger one of my own- the small one started to hurt my wrist as I turned. Once the side supports are screwed in place, you then have to press the racks into the slots. There are three racks that hold three spools, the remaining all hold four, for a total of 57.

This rack is made mainly to hold spools of thread, but you can use it for other things, too. It’s wooden in construction and the first thing I thought of, when I was building it, is how it could be enhanced with wood stain, paint, etc. It looks fine the way it is, but anyone who works with wood will likely think the same thing.

The spinning base on this rack moves freely and easily. The open slots on top are just right for lifting and moving it. My youngster likes to sew and already has filled all of the racks with her spools and other things. It’s practical, looks good, and is made from wood, which is a plus. It makes a great addition to any home crafter’s hobby room.

I have a teenager in the home who loves to make things and her favorite way to make things is with crocheting. Knowing how much she values crochet hooks and accessories, an obvious addition to our household is this Crochet Hooks Kit, made by Houson.

This set includes the more traditional crochet hooks in assorted sizes, plus a set of wooden crochet hooks. But that isn’t all- you also get lots more with this set, like a measuring stick, plastic ring markers, plastic needles, scissors, etc. With your set of basic needs covered, you are all set to grab some yarn and start creating things.

The best thing about the traditional set of crochet hooks is the thick rubber handles. I guess in a way this makes them less traditional, but crocheters will like these handles because they are much more comfortable to hold compared to a straight, thin, metal crochet hook. Some of the other accessories in this kit may be of limited use, but it is helpful to have them, for those times when you need them. And it is nice that the carrying case helps to keep everything organized.

Crochet is made easier when you have the tools for the task and this set of crochet hooks and accessories from Houson is a good one overall. You get lots of hooks and lots of extras so that you have the things on hand, when you need them.

Crafters usually like to get creative. Once you have a completed craft or some other work of art or decoration, you might be looking for ways to make it even better and a good option is to add a little flair using these Tulle Rolls, from Anivia.

With this package of tulle rolls, you get 12 colors. Each roll contains 25 yards, so it should last for a while. The tulle is 6 inches in width, which isn’t all that much. But there is enough here to add a decorative touch to any number of items.

The material used with this is polyester and you can tell it’s polyester when you touch it. I find it perfectly fine- it isn’t like your going to have to feel it against your skin. The netting makes for a cool addition to various crafts and it makes for a nice bow when tied to an already wrapped gift. It can be used anyplace where you think a border would be appropriate. My kids even took a roll and started to decorate the staircase with it! There really is no limit. Only your imagination stands in the way.

The fact that each roll is only six inches in width does result in some limitations. But other than that, you should find these tulle rolls very useful for those times when you want to make something more decorative.

One of my teenagers likes to make things from fabrics. She usually goes to the fabric store to select what she likes, takes it home, and pulls out her needles/threads to see what she can create. Her interest was sufficient to convince me to look for fabrics for her, and that led me to get this quilted fabric from Carmino.

You get seven squares with this kit. I went with the pinkish- colored set. The hue was nice for creating things that require softer colors. The fabric is thinner than I expected and not as high quality as that which we have purchased at the local fabric store, but it’s not bad.

Because there are seven different shades of colors with this set, you are somewhat limited in what you can do with it. The fabric is roughly 16 by 20 inches in size, so each of the included 7 pieces is not that big. You need to keep that in mind when you decide to make something. You have to make sure that what you’re making can be created with slightly different colors and won’t need lots of fabric of one, single color. If you run out of the color you want, you may have a difficult time finding a match at the store. Thus, you are limited when in a pinch.

This fabric is easy enough to use and it works well if you’re making something simple. My kid said it was fine, but she has limited experience. It is likely not sufficient for more experienced quilters, but if you use it accordingly and realize its limitations, you will find that this set of fabrics from Carmino is good for basic needs.

Creating tie dye clothing can be fun, but sometimes it can be quite messy. You have to protect things from the dye, since it can be difficult to remove, and the hot water can lead to burns. But there are ways to easier tie dye and one example is this Tie Dye kit from Lahien.

This kit contains everything you need to create tie dye clothing, plus a little more. You get 26 different colors of tie dye powder, individual bottles to hold the mixed liquid, etc. I expected this, but to my surprise, there are also 8 fabric markers and even disposable plastic gloves. I would have never predicted there was so much packed inside such a small box.

One of the best features of this tie dye kit is that it doesn’t require heated water. Regular, room temperature tap water will do the trick. This is great because, with the heated water method normally required with other tie dye kits, you end up with a large pot full of hot water and dye, and it’s often difficult to clean up afterward. It requires serious scrubbing efforts, but there is no such problem with this kit.

You can make creative tie dye with ease with this tie dye and every creation will be different. We have concentrated mainly on t- shirts, but you can use this kit for other things, too. The patterns it creates are fun and it takes little effort with minimal cleanup. I recommend using only a minimal amount of each color to make the kit last as long as possible. You have 26 different colors, so try to stretch out your use of each, for long lasting enjoyment.

Tie dye patterns are interesting and this tie dye kit from Lahien has everything you need to make tie dye patterns on clothing of different types. Just mix the powder, grab the rubber bands, and let your imagination take control.

One of my teenagers is really into arts and crafts. More than anything, she loves making colorful items using fabrics and she loves to add her own artistic flair whenever possible. To aid in her pursuits, I got her this PONLCY fabric marker set.

The best thing about this set of fabric markers is that you get such a wide array of colors. With 26 different colors from which to choose, you can find a shade to match what you need. And the markers are dual- tipped, with a fine tip on one end and a wider tip on the other. This makes it easier to make the width of marks you want.

I originally got this set of markers with the intent of my teenager using them on clothing, but you can use this on much more than just shirts and pants. They can also be used on shoes, handbags, hats, and the like. It really depends on the material. And to add to the fun, the set comes with four templates with built- in shapes. My kiddo prefers to mark things up her own unique way, but the templates are cool because they provide ready to use shapes, perfect for when you want to create a specific design and would rather not take chances.

I’m not sure how long these fabric markers will last. They are not very big, so they may run out quickly. But PONLCY has come up with a useful set of fabric markers here and they make a good choice of gift for the creative folks among us.

If you like to create your own works of art, certain supplies are essential. You need things to create colors and a good place to start is with markers. A very good set of markers to get you started is Artist Brush Markers Pens for Adult Coloring Books, 34 Colors Dual Tip by Soucolor.

With this set of markers, you get 34 colors. This is enough to satisfy most artists, as there is a shade to match just about any need and I like how the different markers have numbers on them. This way, if you find one you like and use it for a specific purpose, you can be sure to grab the same one again by remembering the number.

The other cool feature with these markers is the dual tip. One end of the marker makes narrow, thin lines while the other makes wider lines. This is perfect for those times when you need a specific thickness and it’s great for teachers because you can distinguish one mark from other- for example, you can use the thicker lines to draw attention to something positive, the thin lines for something negative, etc.

The ink in these markers dries reasonably quickly. In less than a minute, it is dry to the touch. The only thing I wish was different is the carrying container. It’s a plastic bag with nothing inside to keep the markers organized. It would be much better if it came with a tray container with individual slots to hold each marker. Other than that, this is highly useful marker set for teachers, students, and artists.

What value is there in a set of markers? Well, there is in fact plenty of value if the set includes a wide range of colors and other features. And this is what you get with MyLifeUNIT Dual Tip Brush Pens, 36 Colors Fineliner Pens Set for Calligraphy, Drawing and Writing.

I got this set of markers for my junior high schooler and the chief reason she fell for these is the variety of colors. With 36 different hues, you should have no problem finding the right color for the right piece of art. A few of the colors turned out a little different in shade then expected, so I recommend testing each on a plain sheet of scrap paper first, just to make sure the color is what you want.

The other cool feature about these markers is their dual tips. One end of the marker has a thin tip, the other has a broader tip. This is perfect for creating just the right line and I like that the tips are on opposite ends of the same marker. This is better than other sets of fine and broad tipped markers, most of which require two separate markers. With this set, you just flip the marker the other way, remove the cap, and you’re ready to go.

This set of MyLifeUNIT markers comes in its own storage bag so that you don’t lose any markers and can easily carry them from place to place. About the only issue my kid has with these markers is that the colors are not as vivid as she had hoped and they don’t blend together as well as the colors from other marker sets. Other than this, MyLifeUNIT Dual Tip Brush Pens are a good set of markers for artistic and other uses.

My kids love art. My youngest, in particular, loves to create things and she loves art kits of most any type. Her interest in all things artistic was enough to convince me that she would like the Bingo Castle Rocks for Painting, Art Supplies Rock Painting Kit.

With this art kit, kids get 9 small rocks, ready for painting and decoration. There are 12 small containers of paint, stickers, etc. It has what you need to create works of art in whatever creative manner you choose and while it is generally considered something that kids will enjoy, adults may like it, too.

My kid likes this rock art kit and she loved using the paints to make rocks reflective of her personality and mood. She decided not to use the stickers because she felt they hampered creativity. She agreed that the stickers could be useful for those who want them, she just chose not to use them. The paint was all she needed to let her creative reflexes shine.

The included paint containers that come with this set are small, but it takes very little paint to get the job done. As long as you use a little bit of each container and don’t go nuts using a specific color or two, you will find that there is more than enough paint to color every rock in the set with paint to spare. My kid, in fact, started to immediately think of other uses for the remaining paint once she finished.

Budding artists like to paint. They often like painting more than most other artistic endeavors and Bingo castle Rocks for Painting is a very good art kit for creative expression. My kid likes it very much and gives it an enthusiastic recommendation.

I have a budding artist in my home. She likes to work on crafts and loves anything from crocheting to pottery molding and beyond. Painting is one of her favorite activities so I took advantage and quickly grabbed the DIY Oil Paint by Numbers for Adults Painting by Numbers for Kids with Full Set of Assorted Color and Brushes.

This set comes complete with everything you need: brushes, paints, and the canvas. The numbers and colors are clearly marked so you can go to work immediately, filling in the sections of the canvas with the correct, matching color. Once finished, you need to let it dry and can then place it within a frame of your choosing, for display.

I like that this kit comes complete with the brushes and I like that there is nothing else needed to get started. But there are some issues with this set and one is the dark numbers- it takes a good deal of paint to cover them with the lighter colored paints. And like I have noticed others mention, the canvas is folded so you have to work this out in order to make the fold not noticeable. I can’t understand why the manufacturer didn’t think of this when the packaging was decided on. There are ways to avoid this issue, but they went ahead and took the cheapest route for packaging the kit.

This work of art does look cool when completed and of course there is no rule that says you must use the colors in way that is recommended. You could let your creative side go wild and paint any way you like. But it’s best, I think, to follow the guide if your intent is to keep the results and display them.

Art is a favorite hobby of my youngest child and this DIY Oil Paint by Numbers for Adults is a cool way to keep your budding artist occupied. It’s not perfect for the reasons mentioned above but it’s still a good kit overall and the price is right.

Now that's magnificent! It really gives off terrific light and the magnification is fantastic for tiny detailed work. I make a lot of miniatures - so I'm often cutting and gluing tiny bits of things. This helps me keep it precise. When I'm working on the bigger stuff the light is still a huge help for my work table. I love that I can move it easily and it clips to just about anything.

I'm in the process of re-upholstering my sofa - I got the thing stripped down to the frame, and have rebuilt the foundation layers on the seat and back. Now I'm paused in the project while I decide what color fabric to choose for the long term commitment that the next phase will bring. Since it's nice to have a place to sit while I decide... I pulled out an old canvas futon cover and slipped the old seat cushions and some pillows in it and put them on the sofa as temporary cushioning. The futon cover was off white. One day my daughter had stretched a dark purple sleeping bag over the sofa planning to curl up in the sleeping bag... the color looked great! So.. I ordered a two 8 ounce bottles of purple Rit Liquid Fabric Dye and mixed them into 4 cups of very hot water, with salt, following the directions from the web site for dyeing fabrics in the washing machine. It came out looking kind of berry purple - not pink - but not the deep purple like the sleeping bag. So I ordered 3 more 8 oz purple bottles, and one navy blue bottle and followed the directions again - adding all four in the washing machine and tossing the berry colored futon cover in for another round. It came out perfect! A deep purple / violet color. Now I just have to choose a type of fabric in about this color to finish my upholstery project.
Lovely deep red color - this looks so pretty with brown kraft paper wrapping and a burlap sash around a gift.
Great color - very much as pictured - I love wrapping gifts and using products that have a sort of natural texture and look.
These are perfect for crafts!  I had my granddaughters paint "mushrooms" for my gardens and the round sponge brushes did just what I had hoped.  Wash up easy and are reuseable.

I have used these points for personal use and for clients as well. Recently I used them to teach a watercolor workshop. They are fantastic for beginners as well as the seasoned artist. These were just what I needed to convert some earrings from fishhooks to leverbacks. I've had Singer and Kenmore machines before and this Brother was to replace my Kenmore that wasn't working very well anymore. A back door or half enclosed back would also be nice.
I have a few score industrial firebricks laying around, but most might not.
Maybe a handle?

Now all that noted:
This is a nice, solidly made, small forge.
I do one item at the time so for me it's pretty much perfect and, if ever needed, I can purchase the larger version. It's a quality item and it should last a long time. Pretty portable if need be.

I purchased these mainly because my wife wanted the turquoise feather, and this came with two of them. The construction at the 'stem' there the feathers are bundled for each of the ones in this set is a major plus in my opinion. They are bundled and will not easily pull out and get separated. Yes, you can pull them out if you handle them carelessly, but if you treat them as a delicate accessory, they will stay together without problems.

The colors are very vivid and each pair of the same colors perfectly match which leads me to conclude that they are dyed. Not an issue as far as I am concerned.

When they arrived my wife put one in my favorite fedora and I loved it (when I purchased them I did not think that I would also use them.) They add a lot to the look of a hat, and since most of our shared collection of nine hats are fedora style, I will be using the feathers too.

I had to double check the price when I opened and checked out the pieces in the review sample that I received. It's hard to believe that you can purchase this set at about twenty five bucks (at the time of this review).

Since the photos and product description clearly describe what is in the set I'll spare you a recitation. What I will do is cover my observations, and personal likes and dislikes.

Dislikes first: the only thing that I found a little cheap was the BBQ mitt. To be honest, it's kind of flimsy and probably will not hold up. I would have loved to have received a silicone mitt (or set of them). On the other hand, this entire set is priced so low that had I been shopping I would have passed it by because it's too inexpensive.

Likes: first, the fact that the utensils are made from stainless steel is a major reason why I awarded the set five stars. I went straight for the knife to see if it was sharp with a decent blade of the weak link. It's sharp, the blade is sturdy and it's anything but the weak link in this set.

Obviously this set is assembled for folks who cook kebobs and skewered meals. I call into that category, so I am naturally disposed to rate it highly because of that. However, it also has more traditional BBQ tools, so it's versatile enough for the hamburger/hotdog/steak crowd too.

The case is another nice touch and surprise. It sure keeps things organized and is pretty rugged. The zipper is a little on the lightweight side, but should hold up if not abused. I am guessing that the case alone is worth a third of the overall price, so the value is apparent. One feature that I particularly love about the case is for the larger tools the retaining loops are velcro, so you don't have to struggle with removing from the case - or returning them after they've been cleaned.

Overall, this set is a bargain and there were no corners cut on the utensils. I did replace the mitt that came with mine with a pair of silicone BBQ gloves, but still feel that this is a value.

The following is just my opinion. I bought this set because of the case (which is very good) and the other things that came with the pencils.  I was very disappointed in the pencils.  Nearly all of them were scratchy.  (About 7 or 8 were ok.)  I was only able to get about two layers and only a very few burnished. I have a lot better inexpensive sets that lay down buttery and vibrant.  I have cheap sets that are comparable to Prismacolor and some of the other expensive pencils like Holbein. These Cool Bank might be fine for kids of a certain age but adults may like some of the other sets better.  Check out some of the Youtube hosts who review many of the brands on Amazon before choosing a set.  Even the Amazon Basics among some other colored pencils have received good reviews.  Get pencils that are a joy to use.

I was skeptical but I trusted the channel host on Color My World who recommended these pencils.  I have watched several of her videos and she (sorry I don't recall her name at the moment) tests and reviews many color pencil sets.  She demonstrates and instructs so after watching her I developed a lot of confidence in her integrity.  Therefore, I bought these pencils and what a great deal they are.  These pencils lay down soft and the pigments are beautiful.  These are just as good as Prismacolor Premier which I own also.  The only thing that would make these better if they came in a 120, 150, 160, 180 or 200 pencil set.  The price is excellent for these quality pencils.  I have had no problem sharpening these in my cheap battery pencil sharpener as well.  In my opinion, these are certainly worth buying. I use these together with my Prismacolor, Starjoy, Holbein and Brutfuner mostly, whatever set has the exact color I am looking for.  Markart is right up there with choice pencils but at a nice price.  Nobody should complain as you are getting some of the best pencils at a super low price, just compare and you will be amazed.

I have tried other ring guards that fall off quickly,  these wrap around the band of the ring and will NEVER fall off. You can only wrap it around twice if that's what you need! I did that on a pinkie ring. Yeah, no more losing expensive rings!!!

I rarely wore my rings because they would spin around on my finger. I even lost one because it fell off. These are excellent! I had to cut one in half for a looser fit and it's perfect now. So happy i tried these. I  will have to buy more as I have more rings to fit.
