Great for small works or planning larger works in the field, these pads do not have the usual "canvas" paper, but rather actual canvas. Each sheet is gessoed on one side and natural on the other. The edges are glue-bound at the top. They are fastened firmly enough to stay bound if you want to use this as a sketchbook, but the pages can be removed for framing. The first sheet in each book is attached more firmly to the cover, so you lose about 1/4 of an inch on that side. Great for artists who work in small spaces, on the go, or who want to do small acrylic studies. Good also for experimentation, students, teens, and beginners. Would make a nice gift with a starter set of small acrylic tubes, brushes, and accessories.

I love this thing! You have to attach the silicone wrist bands. That's the only part that's tricky. You have to be firm, but once they snap into place, you're ready to go. The top of the unit opens out revealing all the colors you need for sketching. There's even a mini mixing palette and a bit of foam to wipe off your included water brush. This is so clever! It is made of plastic and is light as a feather. The buckled wristband adjusts to many sizes and is very comfortable.

Whether you are headed out on a wilderness backpacking trip, urban sketching in town, visual note-taking on the train, or globetrotting, this is a great little tool. If you are planning to give it to an artist friend. know that it arrives in a beautiful, simple storage box with extra paint pans and a travel-sized water brush.

I love it!

This set of rich, brilliant transparent watercolors comes in a nicely designed narrow metal box. An inside palette folds over the paints so they will stay protected (and unlikely to leak all over your bag) until the next time you need them. The lid (also a palette) folds over the inner palette and snaps securely shut. The box is narrower than most without sacrificing utility. From the included colors, it's easy to mix whatever you need.

I used the set for a Plein air painting in the driver's seat of my car. The included paper is not really cold press. It has more of a linen texture on one side and is smooth on the other. It's soft, and the tape I used to mask the margins pulled off a bit of the surface, but the colors were very bright and the paint kit, including the water brush, was easy to use.

This kit is great for travel or Plein air studies. It's also good for students. If you're a serious painter or you're taking a class, use the included paper to experiment with mixing the paints, layering, and experimentation. You'll want to spring for a better support when you do an actual painting.

This is an excellent deal for the money, The color selection is fine and the paints are vibrant.

If you want to experiment with painting in watercolor on canvas instead of paper, this is a great deal. The canvases are nicely made and mounted on cardboard, sealed in the back. To protect your finished works without glass you might want to seal them with cold wax.
The variety of sizes makes this set useful for Plein air painters as well as students and crafters. This would make a nice gift. The canvas surface is sturdier for lifting mistakes than most paper. It's also friendly to mixed media techniques such as collage and even careful scratching and other subtractive methods. It's much like other canvas panels with a more even finish.
The included sizes fit standard, readily available frames. A good deal, and whether used in the studio, classroom, or outdoors, hours of fun and creativity.

Whether you're making greeting cards, creating a visual journal, or experimenting with collage, this set of little decorative vintage sheets will inspire you. There are reproductions of old stamps, floral patterns, old papers, and more. Use one at a time, cut them up, layer them, combine them with bits from your own drawers. Have fun! They are small, and the the designs are repeated (each one is not unique,) They are reproductions on one side--not original bits of scrap. They are most suitable for journals, greeting cards, and other small projects, but, as I said, they are inspiring.

These sturdy, beautiful stencils are great for crafters, artists, rock painters, scrapbookers, and older children. (They are perfectly safe for younger kids, but the designs are delicate and might not give them the best results.) Use them with inks, watercolors, gouache, acrylics, brushes, sponges, or markers. Create greeting cards, ceramic tiles, or fabric designs. Also good for decorating furniture. Mixed media painters and scrapbookers will have fun incorporating parts of the designs into their works. Uses for these beautiful stencils are limited only by the user's imagination.

If you love drawing with pencil, you need these handy little tools. There's nothing else quite like them. With a light touch, you can transform graphite lines into smooth tones. The banana shape allows you to control the gradient in small areas or to soften large sections of your drawing with ease. Great for illustrators, storyboard artists, portraitists, and more. Keep one on your drafting table, throw one in a bag with your sketchbook, and have one to spare. When they get dirty, rinse them out and let them dry--a very fine synthetic sponge-like material. Should last for years.

For yourself or as a gift for an artist friend, this set of brushes comes in a reusable case that makes it easy to find the one you want. Best for gouache, watercolor, inks, or thinned acrylics, the set includes a useful variety of shapes and sizes, mostly on the small side. They are great for watercolor postcards, illustrations, sketchbooks, and Plein air studies.

These beautiful tapes are great to add borders to any project for graphic designers, illustrators, scrap- bookers, hand-crafted card makers, and other crafters working on paper. These paper tapes are so light they are almost translucent.

These opaque gel pens are so much fun to use on black paper. The gel ink flows smoothly and covers like magic. Artists, illustrators, crafters, and card makers will love them.

Great for yourself or for a gift. I did the abstract drawing included here while talking to my daughter on the phone.

This artist-quality paint has great coverage and rich color. It compares well (and mixes with) other mid-range artist-quality brands. I'm a studio painter and use a lot of acrylics. I will be adding interesting colors from this brand to my collection.

These are not soft-tipped slightly-flexible markers. Each of the beautiful pens has a real nylon brush tip for flowing thick and thin lines. The brushes have great points and are very resilient. The color range is inspiring, and the hinged tin box makes them easy to use, carry, and store. The color-filled are not refillable, but two included water pens are. You can fill those with water and use with pan colors, or to create washes in conjunction with the color pens.

This set is great for students, tweens, crafters, card designers, and multimedia artists. Would make a terrific gift.

Whether you want to design posters on dark-colored surfaces, join the painted rocks craze, decorate ceramics, or add a border to your mirror, you'll find colors in this set to suit your needs.

There are pastel tints that show up on the darkest stones or papers. There are also rich blacks, purples, and browns for light-colored backgrounds. If you need fluorescents to catch attention, there are a couple of pinks, a green, and an orange. There's a nice range of neutrals too.

The paint in the pens has an ink-like consistency and flows into the fiber tip as you use the marker. (You have to press down on a firm surface--not your work--to start it flowing.

These are a lot of fun and would make a great gift for crafters of any age, handmade card designers, mixed media artists, and imaginative teens.

Painting on rocks is fun, but I like smallish flat river stones, and many markers I've tried have points that are too bold for fine detail. This set's tips are extremely fine for paint markers.

The pigments are pastels for a reason. Many rocks are dark and white mixed with the pigments in these pens make the colors show up better. This is also true if you want a set like this to draw or write on dark surfaces.

The opaque paint flows evenly out of these pens as you use them. You have to push down a couple of times on a firm surface (not your artwork) to get the pigment flowing. After that, hardly any pressure is needed. Pract to get a feel for them before working on a "real" project.

These a wonderful for crafters, hobbyests, and mixed media artists. Great fun!

The set includes not only very fine to medium (size 10) pens, but also four brush points with the same ink (XS, S, M, and L). The brush tips make thick and thin lyrical lines fun and easy to do.
The ink is waterproof and doesn't smear under either the watercolor or alcohol markers I tried.
This is a great set for artists and illustrators as well as students.

The soft colored pencils in this set lay down rich, brilliant color. (Better on white and light colors than on black.)
The handsome, well-made zipper case will be a keeper, even when I have worn the pencils down to nubs and need to replace them. Everything is included--sketching pencils up to 4B, soft, medium, and hard square graphite sticks, an eraser, and even a sharpener. Toss this set, along with a sketchpad in your luggage or backpack for making drawings and color notes on your next trip or hike. No water required.

The set is good for artists at all levels and the price is very reasonable.

The beautiful watercolor pens in this set are refillable. This is amazing. No more used-up markers. Grab a small funnel (not provided) unscrew the bottom of the pen and refill the pen with your own watercolor paint.

The pens have soft, flexible, brush heads with actual bristles. They come to a fine point to make thick and thin lines.

Great for calligraphy, sketching, or illustration this set would make a great gift for a student, online-product-designer, or Plein air enthusiast (if you can bear to give it away.) The pens come in four reusable plastic trays neatly designed to help them in order. The trays fit into the plastic box shown in the product photo. There's a handy color chart on the back of the box.

A beautiful, practical set of brush pens for yourself or a friend.

Gouache, long a favorite opaque water-based paint with illustrators and designers, has one major drawback. You can't reactivate it on your palette as you can with watercolor. Once it's dry, it's dry. If you try to rewet it, the quality degrades severely. Hence the unique design of this paint set.

I'm sure you are familiar with commercial individual jelly and jam serving packages served in breakfast restaurants. That same design (only slightly larger) has been used for the paint containers in this set. Each container opens and reseals separately. Take out the paint you need and close the top for your next session.

When you are setting up your palette, turn all of the containers over so you can see the colors. A quick hint--arrange them so your favorites are in the top row. (Most of us only use a few colors in any piece.) Then you won't have to turn them all over each time.

I wish they had colored the top of each jelly cup to match the paint inside or made the tops clear.

If you do precision painting, or you know someone who does, you will love this set of delicate, well-made brushes for craftsmen. If you intend to give them as a gift, they come in a beautiful cylindrical gift box with a black velvet presentation pouch inside. Each brush is separately wrapped and has a rigid plastic sleeve to protect the point. The set I ordered was for my husband who is an HO model railroader. He uses brushes like these to detail tiny people and structures. He was delighted!

If you like the precision of charcoal pencils (as opposed to vine charcoal or sticks) but you find peeling off the paper to expose tips annoying, you'll love these wrapperless pencil-like medium-hardness sharpened charcoal sticks. They look like woodless drawing pencils, but they are charcoal.

They come in a sturdy cardboard tube with a top that slips on and off. It's best to store them in the tube because they are brittle. If you've worked with charcoal, you understand this is inevitable. They are really nice and artists at any skill level will enjoy using them to add detail to drawings or to rough-in small sketches.

They come sharpened to a nice point and the bodies of the "pencils" are sealed with black lacquer (or something similar) to make them a little less brittle and to keep your hands clean.

Don't drop them, and they should last a long time.

In a beautiful metal case with two fold-out mixing surfaces, this set of watercolors is easy to use and maintain. The pans come out so you can move them around, clean them, or even refill them with your own colors after you use them up. Yes, this kit is designed to outlast the paints it contains.

It's not pocket-sized, but it's small enough to toss into a tote for a sketching trip. There's one water brush included, but there's also room for a regular short-handled brush inside.

The 36 included colors are beautiful, quality paints sure to delight any beginner.

Just the right size for more detailed sketches, this paper is great for Plein air excursions as well as in-studio designs, and studies. At 140 pounds, it doesn't buckle when wet and the cold-press texture is satisfying to work on. The glue binding releases easily. (The cover is attached only to the stiff cardboard backing. In the field, you can fasten the active page in place when you mask the edges, dispensing with a drawing board. The less you have to carry the better, right?)

Priced for students, these pads are also good for more experienced artists. Using less pricey materials can lead to taking chances, loosening up, having fun, and even making break-throughs. Use it with watercolors, acrylics, markers, ink, oil pastels, graphite, mixed media, or collage.

Consider this set as part of a gift for budding artists age 10 and up. (Nothing in the set is harmful to younger children, but they would get just as much fun out of cheaper materials.)

This handsome case holds a great selection of colored pencils for easy carrying and storage. The flip pages inside have elastic that keeps the pencils in place. It's easy to arrange them by color so you can always find just the one you need. Even when completely full (I have a lot of pencils) the sturdy zipper closes easily.

It has a short self-fabric handle for easy carrying. Great for travel or for a shelf in your studio. If an artist or illustrator is on your holiday list, this would make a much-appreciated gift. Also good for fans of adult coloring and art students.

I go out once a week in good weather with a group of local Plein air artists. They are serious about it. They set up French easels with umbrellas and paint on canvas with oils.

More power to them, and they are good, but the light changes too fast for me and by the time I get everything set up it's raining, it's 102 degrees, or the wind is carrying off small cars parked along the roadside. I learned how to do ink and watercolor sketches from an online class and I'm happy.

I just pull out my folding stool, grab my kit (in a zipper canvas bag), pick a sketchbook, and go to work. I can finish a color sketch that catches the mood of the day in a little more than 20 minutes. This little sketchbook is the perfect size. It's better quality than I usually use, so the watercolors sing on the paper and I can lift them if I need to. Sometimes I have to resort to gouache at home if things get too dark.

The pages are stitched into the book, so if you get a good one, you'll have to use it for reference. That's okay though. It's fun to have a record of your summer in one place. The paper is heavy enough to use the backs for paintings, or you can make notes on one side and paint on the other.

Great for a gift with a travel palette, if you can part with it.

Five yards of heavy duty hook and loop tape can work wonders in your home office, workshop, garage, laundry room and more. attach a piece of the hook tape to the item you want to hang up and a piece of the loop tape to your wall the side of your bookcase, or anywhere else you want to hang the item.

Use it to keep remotes at hand, tools handy, and more. This stuff is really strong.

This beautiful set comes in an amazing gift pouch. It has everything a new watercolor artist will need even eight sheets of sketchbook sized paper. There are 36 generous pan colors, along with a tube of white (in case you need touchups.). The included water brush and sketching pencil fit right inside the tin box. There's also a small regular watercolor brush for details.

This kit is great for people new to plein air painting as well as students and children. If your kids or grandkids are crazy with bordedom this summer, cue up some watercolor lessons online and grab one of these sets. All you need to add is a cup of water. (You'll need more paper too. Watercolor or mixed media paper work best. Copy paper is too lightweight. It will buckle and pucker.)

Whether you're headed out to do a color sketch in the wilds or working on an illustration project in your studio, this set of watercolor pencils is sure to meet your needs.

If you use a sturdy paper, you can wet the surface and draw on it for rich colors. You can also wet the pencil tips and draw that way, or just use these like regular colored pencils. They are softer and lay down a lot of water-soluble pigment. They are great for mixed-media projects. Unlike other sets, this contains no repeated colors. There's a handy list of hues with swatches inside the case lid if you want to reorder.

Speaking of the case, the top plastic tray lifts out and fits in the lid when you are working so you can grab the right pencil without fumbling. I don't really see the logic of the color arrangement as the set comes, but I think artists will want to set up the pencils in ways that suit them.

This beautiful set would make a great gift for any artist, student, or hobbyist,

White touches can make or break designs. On dark surfaces, pure whites can be hard to achieve. These white acrylic paint markers are the solution. Once activated (easy), the white acrylic paint flows smoothly through the firm fibrous tip delivering a beautiful white line that keeps its brilliance.

For example, I was playing with Arteza's new black paper DIY frame sketch pad and created a texture with watercolor pencils. (Dumb, I know, now...anyway...) The result was just awful, but I didn't want to waste that beautiful paper, so I drew the white line design over the top with one of these pens. It's not Matisse, but the pen performed brilliantly.

If you do multi-media work, you'll love these (and be grateful to have plenty of them.) There's only one thing. Activating the pens is easy. You just put them on a hard surface (a palette is good) and press down until the paint starts to flow. Notice, I didn't say to do this on your artwork. The first time, there will be a puddle (and you can use a brush to keep from wasting it, if you want.)

If you make my mistake and have a puddle on your work, try to lift it off. On non-absorbent surfaces, too thick a layer will crack when it dries. This can be interesting, of course, but probably not what you had in mind for your project. Normal white acrylic lines from these pens are smooth, even, and do not crack. (You have to press really hard to make a puddle. Don't worry about it. It won't happen when you are drawing.)

The pens, once activated, stay ready for use. Just be sure to screw the top back into place as soon as possible. If you've worked with acrylic, you know its advantage and disadvantage is its fast drying time.

Low odor, great coverage, easy application. What's not to love? You need these in your studio or hobby cubby.

I LOVE this elastic! It is soft, stretchy, and comfortable. So far I have repaired one of my husband's masks and the goggles he wears to work on his hobby projects. It is the perfect width and weight. A TON of it comes on this pro-sized bobbin, so if you are making masks for everyone in your family and half the neighborhood, you will have plenty to work with.

I attached a replacement band of this elastic to my husband's mask with regular staples, but I could have stitched it by hand or with the machine. It's easy to sew through, pin, tie, or, as I said, even staple. With surges in the virus (as well as seasonal flu) possible in the fall, now is the time to lay in a supply of safety gear. (There's a cool tutorial online fo making a face shield out of a 2 liter soda bottle. You could use this elastic for that too.)

Of course, If you are an accomplished seamstress and make your own lingerie, this elastic is great for that too! Highly recommended.

we use it to make little figures which is fun, but the product has a droop effect so you really need to know how to use it else it can be very frustrating. I wanted a variety of colors so I branched from my normal brand, but I will be returning to my brand that can actually be used to sculp.
I oen a cameo silhoutte and after trying everything under the sun for transfering and having lots of issues. A friend recommended this product. It is a game changer. Works like a charm everytime.

I really wanted to like this because I really really want to start working with clay again but finances are terribly tight right now. So, at such a good price, I had hoped this would be perfect for me to play around a bit and relearn some of the old techniques and such I once knew. For starters, this thing is SOOOOOO TINY! I'm talking smaller than like a car battery cut in half or like a bag of sugar that's had the top third cut off. That part though, is my bad. I should have paid more attention to the sizes. The little pack of tools that come with are super small, flimsy, and of course plastic. The "jewelry pieces" are pretty lousy quality but I didn't expect anything in this set to be amazing. It was only 27 bucks after all. Everything thus far I take the blame for because I should have paid more attention. What I will NOT take responsibility for is the clay itself. This stuff is just AWFUL. It's hard, dry, crumbly and even after working it for like 30 min AND warming it up by placing it in a zip lock baggie and running warm water over it, it just would not come to a proper consistency. It's not usable. The fact that this set is basically meant for young children is insulting because if I, a 48 year old adult, can't get the clay to the proper consistency.... a child would have a very hard time actually playing with it. Overall, it's cute, its small, it comes with a ton of tiny different colored bars of clay, but it's unusable because the clay is awful. And if you can't use the clay, it's worthless. Hard pass on this one!

I can never go back to standard resin curing again. This mat helps to cure my resin pieces in record time so no more waiting a day before I can actually unmold my pieces and then having to let them set up longer to harden. By curing my pieces with this mat I can unmold my pieces within about 2 hours and once they are cool, they are solid with no bending. I am so happy and so impressed with this curing mat. I wish I had this when I first started with resin.
The only fuss I have with this whole kit is the cover and how I wish it had some kind of plastic or metal inside of it in order to help keep the cover open without trying to fold back into itself. However, I am so in love with this curing mat, I will figure something out to make it easier to use because if you use resin, you need one of these mats. Works perfectly!

These are perfect size for making nightlights or small table lamps. The molds are perfect and are easy to fill and use in my pressure pot. The dice come out pretty easily and are ready to go again. Super happy with these.
So far I am in love with this curing machine. It is easy to use and cures my resin pieces in hours instead of a day.

While I love the look of these I have a huge problem with how easily they are to break when trying to attach them to pieces. This mica powder is great and works great in resin. I've been using it here lately doing Christmas ornaments and pieces. The green is beautiful and mixes in resin perfectly. Very happy with this mica powder.

Not only did my resin cure so much faster after putting it in this machine but it cured clearer and much more even too. This machine was easy to use and holds a good amount of items. It's become a necessity now and I don't know if I could ever go back to not using one of these.

bars are small, but a wide variety and very fast shipping.  Will buy again

I am very happy with my order of the Magicdo Mica Powder. This mica powder set and accessories arrived to me in great condition. It is very considerate and thoughtful that this set also includes a spoon and tweezers. I like the flakes, as they work especially well in layered epoxy resin pours. Magic Red is my favorite color included in the set, but they are all really vibrant and most coordinate very well together. I have used this powder set in some resin projects and also have used it with acrylic paints and mediums. A very small amount of the powder goes a very long way. I am very pleased with my order of the VARWANEO 5000PCS Nails for Pearl Setting Machine Pearl Rivet Buttons. These arrived well packaged in a thick, tough, clear re-sealable bag. I did not know what these were initially called, and had a little difficulty finding a listing. I'm so glad that I learned these pieces are called pearl rivets. These are not at all available in any of my local art/craft/sewing stores. I use these with my pearl setting machine that I also ordered from Amazon. The pearls that I use are plastic pearls, sometimes called no-hole beads. Although the beads will vary by size, the rivets that set them do not. These rivets have prongs that go through the fabric and press into the plastic pearl/bead. I primarily use these as buttons when I make doll clothing. Each pearl I set will need a rivet. I am happy with the quantity in this set as I can use around 5,000 no-hole beads. I do hope this supply will last me a long time, but I find these rivets are a very affordable sewing notion. I hope my experience with the rivet set is helpful as you kindly click the button below!

I work with all uncured resin in my garage as I can achieve appropriate ventilation by leaving the big door open and using a fan to exhaust the gasses from mixing, pouring and curing epoxy. I never use resin inside my home as the ventilation system is contained and shared between all of the rooms.

The epoxy in this set is a shallow coating type, so it performs better in shallow, thin poured layers. I do really like the fine glitter and the small jars that contains it. The pipettes are incredibly useful for using both for decorating with small mix-ins like tinted epoxy to make designs and especially to accurately remove air pockets and bubbles. The alphabet mold is soft and flexible, but not too thin or too thick to prove difficult to remove the cured castings. I like to use a silicone mat to protect my work surface and clean-up is easy using baby-wipes and isopropyl alcohol.

I find the set to overall be a fair value. I do wish the safety information was easier to understand as epoxy resin is only non-toxic when it is mixed appropriately and fully cured. The off-gassing and exothermic reaction can pose a hazard to some users. I absolutely do not recommend this be used around children, pets or anyone unable to self-protect. I am happy with my order of this Alcohol Ink Set. If I want to thin the colors, I use a high concentration ethanol (Everclear) and for cleanup I like to use isopropyl alcohol. They also work well in brush pens. There are a couple ways I do not recommend to use alcohol inks, which I found from trial and error. They do not work well with UV curing resin, as the pigments are not all light-fast and cause discoloration. They also do not flow well on porous surfaces such as wood, they tend to bleed. I use all resin products outside my home's ventilation system, in my garage with the door open and a fan to exhaust air outside or under a vent hood. Clean up is not difficult, for mixed resin you can let it cure and remove it from most surfaces like your silicone mat or peel from plastic cups. Isopropyl alcohol and baby-wipes are also great to use for clean-up.

I had good results from the UV Resin Crystal Clear Hard Type Resin. I find this to be a good value for the quantity of product and the included tools. My 100 bottle was securely packaged and included 2 silicone stick tools and 2 small silicone mixing pots. I like these as they can be cleaned with isopropyl alcohol and reused. I used my resin with a 36 watt lamp to make 1cm beads in a clear silicone mold. This took 3 minutes of exposure. This is a good resin from my experience, but only for those that are familiar with the limitations of UV curing resin. It does not need mixing, as it's prepared in the bottle. It is essential that the piece you wish to cure can have light penetration. This is why it's essential to use a clear silicone mold for casting. I also advise to avoid inclusions that would prevent light exposure throughout your piece. It gives the best results for very shallow casting. It's ideal to dome jewelry projects. The included pamphlet has a tutorial for users to use the resin with open-back pendents. I can understand user confusion as this has 'epoxy' in the title. This is a completely different formula compared to epoxy resin, and is not appropriate to use the same way.

This would be a 5-star product rating as it meets all my expectations aside from one. I like to review included literature and safety information for each new product I use. There is only a small amount of this info on the back of the package, but it is not enough. This should be used with proper personal protective equpment, at a minimum with eye protection and chemical resistant gloves in a very well-ventilated area. I cannot locate a Safety Data Sheet for this resin. I cannot even find the name of the manufacturer or any contact information listed on the bottle or packaging to request one. There must be one on file somewhere, but I cannot access it. I find this troubling and concerning as it can cause new/unfamiliar users to take unnecessary risks. As I am familiar with using uncured resin, I have a prepared workspace. I use this while wearing chemical resistant gloves, cover my exposed skin, use a respirator and eye protection. My workspace is in my garage, which does not share a ventilation system with the rest of my home. I have good ventilation in the garage with the door open and a fan extracting air to the outside. I did not note any lingering odors from the cured beads I made with the resin. Lingering odors is a sign the piece is not thoroughly cured.

I did not have bubbles, I used a 5ml pipette and allowed all air to travel to the top of the pipette before putting the resin into my bead mold. There is not a limit to the pot time of UV resin as long as it's not exposed to UV radiation. Bubbles will only occur if they are placed into a piece. If light cannot penetrate a casting, it will not cure. The wrong mold or too many inclusions/additives will cause a cure to fail. If a mold is cast in too thick layers, it will shrink and a user will experience curing inhibition. The curing process causes an exothermic reaction. This is important to know so users can allow their castings some time to cool before handling. It's also important to be mindful of humidity and temperature for best results. I did my castings with 45% humidity and with a temperature of 72°F. I hope that future packaging will include more safety information, or a link to easily access this. I would strongly discourange this to be used by children and anyone else unable to self-protect. I hope that my experience is helpful to you and you kindly will click the button below.

I really like the Twotrees Totem S Laser Engraver. It has some similarities and differences compared to my other small laser engraver. The working space is about 30cm square, so it needs a good, level steady surface all it's own. The assembly was nothing that was too difficult, but it does need to be accurate. I referenced some videos on their website as some of the instructions were a little fuzzy to me.

There are only one pair of glasses included with the laser. I'm not sure exactly what parameters are necessary for proper protection, as that information is thus-far lacking. I'm trying to contact the company to find this information so I may purchase 2 more pair of protective laser glasses. I do wish additional glasses were included. Protecting your eyes from the light is very important, damage can be permanent and I do wish that more through safety information was included. The laser should never be operated around anyone that is unable or unwilling to protect themselves.

I also separately purchased another cable to connect to my computer. I do not want the laser plume to be released inside my home's ventilation system. I will operate the laser in my attached garage where I can vent the plume outdoors. Some materials we might like to engrave can let off some nasty vapors and gasses that could cause allergic reactions. Dust filters in our homes' HVAC unit will do nothing to dissipate these vapors and can cause a safety hazard.

The only reason I cannot award this laser a 5-star rating is due to the lack of safety information, which is incredibly important.

The high points of this laser engraver is the generous working surface and the fact that it is compatible with free, open-sourced software. Plenty of user guidance is available online and there are numerous community groups that are happy to help on the very, very popular social media site. I highly recommend anyone considering a laser engraver to join at least one group, peer user support can be the difference between successful projects and frustrations. I'm still learning the software, but overall it's quite straight forward for this application.

The materials used for the laser and frame are good quality. This part was a pleasant surprise to me, as the price is quite good for the quality. This gives me good reason to recommend this laser engraver to anyone that wishes to take the time to incorporate laser engraving to their hobby skill-set. With proper care and maintenance, I predict this laser engraver will be operating for several years. It's a fair to good value, the operation is not overly complicated and the assembly may be a little difficult for a beginner, but with patience, it should go smoothly.

The set is as it's listed. The WEVOVE Chess Board Mold for Resin is a thin silicone casting mold set for resin. The board measures 11" by 11" and the pieces are familiar sized, for solid castings. The piece molds will need some sort of reinforcement for pouring, like paper tubes, to keep them upright during the curing process. Upon examination, the molds are all smooth on the inside. There are 4 pawns, 1 each of rook, bishop, knight, king and queen. The 4 pawn pieces will need to be cast 4 different times, the same with the rook, bishop, and knight to make a complete set. The king and queen will need to be cast twice. If these are to be tinted, it is important to be vigilant with the color mediums so each player's pieces will match for a smooth game.

Since this has some small spaces deep in the molds, this is an ideal set for pressure casting, or possibly for very slow curing resin to allow the bubbles to be completely released for solid chess pieces. Pipettes are included, and should prove to be handy while casting alternating colored squares on the board. All in all, this is a nice set of molds, that may take a little extra work to cast than other styles. However, having a customized chess set would likely be a very fun project for most resin hobbyists.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope my insight and experience is helpful to you and you kindly will click the button below.

This is a single-action, meaning the trigger controls the rate of paint flow only, the air flow is consistent from the compressor. I find this is a good style for those that are very new to airbrushing or those that use the brush for a material the is consistent in viscosity. An in-line 1/8" flow regulator can easily be added to lower the rate of air flow. You can also slightly adjust the tail adjustment screw for small adjustments to the needle that affects the flow of your paint. The cup is slanted towards the needle and smooth inside, so no paint will be left behind in the cup once it's all expelled. The needle is 0.3 mm size. This is the most popular size, for versatile uses and thought of as a mid-sized needle. The compressor makes some noise, but it's not anything compared to traditional style airbrush compressors. The noise level is comparable to the volume that most would watch television. It would be a good option for those living in close quarters with others.

The included instructions are easy to follow for basic procedures. It is lacking in details such as detailed guidance for a complete break-down of the airbrush itself for deep cleaning and lubrication. I also did not find any extra o-rings, which after a bent needle, is the most likely cause of failure. These would need to be purchased separately in the event. Basic cleaning brushes, a small wrench for the needle nozzle and a single pipette are also included with the re-charging cord that is compatible with USB adapters. The box is suitable for safe storage.

As I do more delicate airbrushing, I find this model would also be suitable for cosmetics or decorating baked goods. I would not advise anyone to use any airbrush for mixed purposes, as art supplies will contaminate edible and cosmetic materials. Cosmetic materials will also contaminate edible materials. For example, once this is used for art supplies, this should be the dedicated use of your airbrush. It's also always a good practice to wear a dust mask when using any airbrush as your liquid material is being atomized by the brush and surrounding surfaces should be covered to avoid over spray. Overall, this airbrush is very safe to use with some very inexpensive, simple and easy precautions.

I find the Anesty AHP1 High Pressure Cordless Airbrush Kit to be a very good value. The ease-of-use, easy maintenance, low noise, easy safety precautions and price point allow this to be a solid 5-star product. Thank you for reading my review. I hope that my experience is helpful to you and you kindly will click the button below.

The Reusable Nicpro Silicone Resin Measuring Cups and Tool Kit is a great kit for helping you to keep yourself and your space tidy. This also saves us from making more trash by avoiding disposable cups and the smooth silicone surface allows more resin to slide into a mold, so a two-fold way to avoid waste when crafting. I don't fine the finger cots useful, I use full gloves along with all the other safety gear when I use uncured plastic resin. You can mix this into the resin, or use them to foil-leaf your project once it's cured using a foil leaf adhesive. Th Xileyw Watercolor Paint Set is a nice little set of half-pans with 48 colors. I found the colors to be very vibrant. Most are opaque except for the metallic, which include too much mica to be anything but opaque. The paint blooms reasonably well, blending and layering caused no surprises. I like the color chart being on the inside lid of the outer case. The inner tin is a little difficult to open, but stays shut quite tight so it's less likely to pop open accidentally. One small brush is included inside the tin with a small cleaning sponge. There are no extra wells in the lid for mixing, so you may want your own water color palette for that. A small packet of papers are also included along with a small box including a second small brush and two brush pens. The brush pens open by twisting them the opposite direction than you are accustomed to. I recommend using distilled water, it's not expensive and available at nearly all grocery stores. This allows for better colors and lessens the instance of microbes colonizing in your paint set.

I cannot give the set a 5 star rating because it's not a good value overall. There are other sets that are just as nice or better at a better price point than this one. Although there are 48 colors, they are very small, even for half pans. The metallic colors are only watercolor paints with a bit of mica powder mixed in, and don't really add a lot to the experience, they only make the colors more opaque, which leaves blooming and blending a little harder to achieve.

I would not strongly discourage anyone from this set. The colors are vibrant, and the variety of colors are pleasant. This would be better suited for hobbyists, lower level students and as a novelty gift. This would not be suited for commission work or upper level students, but it's not advertised as such. The Maxdee 24 Colors Acrylic Paint & 8 Paint Brushes makes a great set for beginners. Select your own canvas and you are all set to paint! The paints are all smooth and consistent. I like to do some messy projects, but the clean-up is much easier thanks to Heflashor 3 Pcs Silicone Sheet for Crafts. Dyvicl Watercolor Paint Set is not a bad little set, but it is very small. For half-pans, these are small. This may be a good or negative point for you. If you do very small studies, with limited storage space, then this will work in your favor. If you want to work on large sheets of paper, you may come up short on your colors. The solid colors are more opaque than I would usually expect from watercolors, but you can get a lot of coverage for them, by re-wetting on your paper, you can layer easily. The greens are very nice.

The case is metal, and opens easily on a set of hinges. You get 3 watercolor pen brushes, these open by twisting the opposite way than you would expect. I only use distilled water with these, to avoid the additives from my municipal water supply affecting my products. I recommend this practice, it's not expensive and available at any regular grocer. The pen brushes are great for smaller palettes. In the case you can find 5 small wells for mixing. The color chart is on the back of the box and is nearly accurate to the colors of the set.

I'm not happy with the performance of the metallic colors. This is mica powder added to the paint, one would be better off to use mica powders separately as it causes issues with blending and gets stuck into the brush bristles. It's a little awkward to clean the brush after using these pans, and the colors of these paints are more pale than I would like.

I don't find this set to overall be a good value. The presentation and idea are nice, but the execution is not so much. I would not highly recommend this set as it is, I've used others in the same range with better performance. I hope this review is Helpful to you, and you kindly will click the button below.

The eraser doesn't smear and the tiny paddle of paper lets me get my pencils down to a very sharp or slanted tip if I need them to be. The blending paper blunts are great as they come in different sizes. The pencil extender did not grip any of the pencils, but this is such a minor negative I do not believe this should deserve a lower rating. I also cannot verify if this set is indeed non-toxic and conforms to ASTM D-4236, so I I cannot strongly recommend this for children or award the set 5-stars. I had fun with it though, and find the price to be fair for what you get. This isn't a very fancy set, but it turned out to be much nicer than I expected for budget-grade materials. Thanks for reading my review. If it was Helpful in any way, please click the button below.

LymGazell 24 Colors Skin Tones Set Art Markers feature all the colors I've always found lacking in other marker sets. There is a white color that shows up well for me, even on white paper (yay!), even though it's suppose to be a blender marker. Their are colors from white, milky to tawny and dark brown. It's best to make your own swatches before starting on a project, using the same paper you intend to use for your work. Whatever color I always wish I had on hand, I now have. I did an experiment, and even found to be able to get a color to mottle without separating pigments with 91% isopropyl alcohol spray, which was really cool and something I have always struggled with when it comes to markers. I cannot say how much surface area you can cover with a single marker, but none have started to dry on me yet. They are duel tipped. As with nearly all markers, I suggest to store them on their side. They come in a nice nylon zippered case, which I appreciate very much. I cannot detect an odor while using them.

There is no safety information available for this marker set. There is no indication this set is ASTM D-4236 compliant, therefore, I cannot award this set a 5-star rating. I would recommend this set to adults only, low-level students and hobbyists. I cannot recommend the set for children, higher level students or professional artists.

60 Colours Watercolor Dual Brush pens by Hohuhu comes in a thick plastic case with 2 snap buttons on the front and a handle on the top. It opens envelope style to reveal 3 trays of markers. There are no safety warnings, no indication that this set is ASTM D-4236 compliant, therefore I cannot recommend these for use by children. For this reason alone, I cannot rate this product at 5-stars.

I do not believe these markers are water-based. They behave more like alcohol ink, but do not have an odor. It will take more testing on my part than I care to do immediately to determine this for certain. They behave like alcohol based markers, the dry time is minimal as is the bleeding/blooming. The dual tips are the biggest highlight of this set. This makes them nice for outlines or for filling in spaces. Multiple layers will get a more saturated color. The brush tip is interesting. This is my first encounter with 'brush' tipped markers. Only the last 2mm of length behaves like a brush, but the effect does look the shape of brush strokes. It's a novel and interesting concept that I enjoyed. I found the handles especially easy for me to hold.

The variety of colors are nice. They are arranged in a sensible order, but there is no color chart. Other than the color of the dyed plastic handle, the colors are not differentiated in any way, not by numbers or names. This leaves the user to make their own swatches from scratch to use for reference, and to keep the order of the markers precise, or number them yourself. I don't care for this, but it is what it is. Each marker preformed well, they are ready for use from the package. I find the colors to be very vibrant, although some are very, very close to each other.

Overall, I find this set to be a fair value. I do find the price to be very economical for the amount and variety included in the set. I would recommend this set to adults only, low-level students and hobbyists. I do not recommend these for children, upper-level students or obviously, professional artists. I'm overall happy with this set, and will continue to use them as they last. I hope you found my review Helpful, and will kindly click the button below.

The thing I really can't emphasize enough is the variety of colors. The description photo is enhanced, but I cannot get the colors to photograph very well, so let's stick with the promo pic. It's not too far off from accurate, but as you know, mica powder is the shiny bits in lipstick, candles, soap and baked goods that make is shiny. I personally wouldn't recommend this for baked goods though, because it's a crafting item. I cannot find a safety data sheet for the powders, but I'm actually not too concerned with this as I'll be wearing full gear anyhow when I do a significant amount of atomized airbrushing, (applying the powder with a brush or sponge after airbrushing) sealing and especially when working with un-cured resins. I'm not going to use this for cosmetics because I cannot find a safety data sheet (SDS), but I didn't ask for one either. I would recommend that you have a SDS for anything you want to be assured is body safe. The minerals that are used in mica powder are harmless, but I cannot say for sure what pigments are used. What I can say is, the colors are really amazing. I use mica powders to mix in paint, to make my dolls look like they are wearing makeup, and contained withing layers of nail gel. I'll be mixing resin soon, so it's going in there as well. This is a great supply of powders, 50 different colors, each gorgeous. This is an incredible value. I would never purchase the overpriced and under pigmented craft store powders, these are much nicer, more variety and much more affordable. Each color is contained in individual re-sealable bags, the lot is also in a larger re-sealable bag in a black branded box for Muerk, so it stores really well and wouldn't spill as long as it's in a safe place away from children and pets. I would strongly recommend this set if you are considering using mica powders.

I find the Craft Evolved - Candle Making Kit to be a great starter set. Included you will find 2 types of wax, 1.5 pounds of soy, and 1/2 pound of soy pillar wax. If you want to make a pillar, you will need to use the mold for this. You have the option of using the included plastic tea lights or small tins. Wicks are provided for each, along with dye, scented oil and several other accessories. The dye colors are not labeled, but the scented oils are. I really like the jasmine. The only thing you need to provide is a sauce pan to boil water to place your pouring pot into to melt the wax. The pouring pot is very nice, thicker than most and has a handle that should hang over the side of your own saucepan.

If you decide to continue to make candles, many of the included items are re-usable, like the melting pot, thermometer, tins and wick holder sticks. With repeated use, you will just need to replenish your supply of wax, containers, stickers, wicks, dye and scented oil for candles. So, your largest one-time purchase is usually the starter set but it's priced much lower than purchasing each item individually. This proves to be a good value.

Simple instructions are included in the box and there is a QR code that is suppose to give access to video instructions. The QR code sent me to Facebook messenger, then to join their Facebook group. Craft evolved does have a website, so you can simply search that term and find it yourself. At the time of this writing, I can only find videos on their site for a different crafting product. I'm quite annoyed at this process to try to see the video content and to come up empty-handed. I did manage to find 2 videos on their company Facebook page. There is also an introductory one on this product description page, but it's a brief overview and shows some more advanced projects. Otherwise, this is a nice starter set and would make a good gift for someone else, or yourself.

If you like the look of steam-punk and crafting, the BIHRTC 140 Gram DIY Assorted Gears may be right up your alley. These are randomly packaged in a little re-sealable bag by weight, and organized by color. Mine are bronze. I set them out to count, I have 89 pieces in 32 different patters. All of mine are gears, 2 have some heart patterns in the gears. They vary in diameter from 1cm to 2.5cm. A few, maybe 4, have some flashing left from the casting process. The pieces are solid and heavy. This may be a pro or con for you depending on intended use. I'd like to put them in some resin pieces, so this is great for me. These are costume grade, so they are suitable for scrap-booking, jewelry, costume accents, sculpture, resin, multi-media art, wherever you want some heavier metal pieces to serve as accents. I highly recommend this bundle for those uses.
As long as you understand from the description that this is a random assortment, you should not be disappointed. I find this to be a fair to good value with realistic expectations.

I really like the Dylviw 9 Pieces Fine Tip Detail Painting Brushes. I paint my own resin ball jointed dolls, doll house miniatures and do small-scale paintings. This set is great for me because of the handle shape. Sometimes smaller brushes are difficult for me to hold due to arthritis in my hands. I can maintain control due to the triangular shaped handle. This is also a great variety of sizes of round brushes, but these are not true liner/riggers, so you can only do short continuous lines. The bristles are trimmed well and the ferrules are tight to the handle. These are easy to clean, as I stick to water-based mediums. As long as you are sticking to details of 2mm or larger, this will work well. In extremely small scales, this will be more challenging, but can be done with a very fine touch with a thinner, highly-pigmented paint.

I own a few airbrushes. I really wish these were available decades ago. This is ideal for smaller projects with paint, cosmetics or decorating baked goods. Just don't go from paint to cake decorating, as most paints are not safe for food consumption.

I use airbrush for a few different paint and sealant applications such as painting my custom dolls, miniature furniture and fine art work I do as a hobby. I also like this a lot for layering sealant and some types of glue, because it's not something that takes me more than a minute to do, and getting out my bigger compressor and setting it up really slows down what should be a simple part of a project. Sealing charcoal drawings with a water-based sealant means I can work in the medium no matter the weather. Before, I had some canned sealant, but that is very sensitive to environmental temperature and humidity.... and it was also toxic, so I couldn't use it indoors at all.

The Anesty Cordless Automatic Airbrush Kit preforms exactly as I expected. It's small, and does not take much storage or work space. It's easy to clean, packs away nicely in it's own little foam lined box and the internal battery is rechargeable with it's own USB cord. It comes with some charge, and it's best to store with a full charge to help the battery last as long as possible. I'm very satisfied with this order.

My advice to using an airbrush is to make sure your medium is safe for use as an aerosol, alcohol ink is not, some paints, dyes and stains also contain a solvent that should not ever be used as an aerosol. Mediums like watercolor, acrylic and most water-based mediums will be fine, just check your product's safety data sheet first. Learn to 'tear down' your airbrush for through cleanings before you use it, this is a great way to be able to trouble-shoot issues before you are struggling in the middle of a project. Mediums you use may need to be thinned, the consistency of milk is the goal. A clean, properly lubricated airbrush should last a very, very long time with proper care.

The only con I can find with the Anesty Cordless Automatic Airbrush Kit is that other airbrushes I own are not compatible with the compressor. This may or may not matter to you, but I found it worth mentioning. That's not something that would prevent me from recommending this kit. It's a great little kit. This is very economical for small projects that don't require a lot of working time.

Vkey's 10mm Diameter Transparent Sticky Back Thin Clear Dots are more than I may ever need as there is over 500 pair, but these are working great for me to keep some doll wigs stay put on dolls. Before, they would slip off easily and I didn't have a good way to keep them on without glue, which I refuse to do. The hook-and-loop dots are ideal as they already have adhesive on them. This way, I can re-dress my dolls without worry or mangling their delicate wig styles. I consider this set a great value as the local shops offered only a few pieces for more. I'll likely find uses for the rest with time as the adhesive can go on a variety of surfaces. I've seen them used at my place of work to hold temporary laminated signage, to close folders and for DIY learning tools for kids. I find this set to be a fair value and the quality as I expected. I would recommend this product to anyone that needs some hook-and-loop dots that already have their own adhesive applied.

KIWIHUB Colored Masking Tape was great. I like the variety of colors as this was handy when masking off some trim in a few rooms that needed different paint used, so I color-coded the masked areas for an easy way to remember what paint is going where. It didn't peel off any wall paint. It has a generous amount on each roll, so I had plenty left for another use. I would highly recommend the masking tape, and the colors lend it for alternative uses when you need some temporary color coding for games with kids, or remembering what paint goes where for a project similar to mine!

The measurements are accurate as far as I can measure, but seeing it in person really hits home. I love this mat, it covers my entire work surface so I can keep things clean and tidy. Since it's silicone, it's heat resistant, so I'm not afraid of marring a surface with a heat gun, iron, or glue gun. It's tacky enough that it holds containers in place and catches stray scatters of powders or small items so they don't become lost to the floor.

I like that it's thin enough to fold to a small size for storage, but not so thick that I fear tearing the material. This is great for so many purposes and uses when I'm doing arts and crafting projects. My only regret is not finding a nice sized mat like this years ago.

Thanks so much for reading my review. I hope it was helpful to you and you will click the button below.

This is a 2-part epoxy resin. I would love to detail the results of a project made with it, but I cannot. Please, let me explain. There is a small booklet inside the package with measuring instructions. There is no information about the manufacturer, or company that markets this product. At first, this wasn't alarming, as it could be a new company or a beta test of a new product. I requesting more information so I could review the Safety Data Sheet..... crickets. So, I examined the logos on the bottles. They come from 2 different international chemical companies. I have no reason to believe either of those companies are affiliated with this product. I could not find it in either or their catalogs, nor do they sell products directly to consumers. Neither companies are located in China, as this product's Amazon label on the plain brown box states.

The bottle states no VOCs.... but I'm not inclined to believe anything printed on a bottle from a company that cannot or will not provide a Safety Data Sheet. Even when that safety information is available for 2-part epoxy products, it applies to each part of the product, separately, and does not apply to any of the chemical reactions resulting from the mixing or curing process. 2-part epoxy products made and sold my legitimate companies have printed warnings, and will feature some indication that their product conforms to to ASTM D-4236 on the packaging, even if it's a non-toxic product. Most art supplies will have this information... this one is completely lacking.

I'm unsure how to use this product safely. No information is available to guide me, and I find this not worth the risk. I would love to create with this, but until the information is available, I will be holding off. As an Amazon Vine reviewer, I take my role as a consumer advocate very seriously. For these reasons, I will not and cannot recommend this product. I strongly suggest you look for a different product that identifies itself as being ASTM D-4236 compliant and offers consumer support for information in regards to safety. Despite the bad news from my review, I hope you have found it Helpful to you and will click the button below. Thank you for reading.

The SEVENWELL 48 Box Shinning Nail Glitter Sequins are thick and stiff compared to most nail art embellishments. They are also very firm and not flexible, so they are better suited for crafting projects, in my opinion. Each set has some variety in the shapes. For example, the pink box includes a butterfly sequin where the other colors do not have that. There is a music note in all 4 of my colors, but mine that is suppose to look purple has a lot of green to the iridescent surface. The sizes of the shapes all vary, while the solid dot is about 2mm, the music note is 10mm long.

I'm very happy each different sequin is inside it's own threaded box. There are 4 color collections separated into their own carton with a window punched out. This way, you can keep the collection inside it's box for better organization. I like these because I've wanted some long diamond shapes to make some doll eyes for a resin cat doll I have. I've been looking for approximately 2 years to find a glitter or sequin that would be small enough to be a pupil, and I'm thrilled to finally have found not only one color, but 4 different ones!

This is certainly a very fun set that I look forward to sharing with my local group of friends when we do DIY crafting meetups. I would recommend this set for it's value and variety of color, shapes and smart packaging. Thank you for reading my review. I hope you have found it Helpful and will click the button below.

This is a nice comprehensive set of watercolor round brushes from Fuumuui. They sable bristles are nice and subtle, so you can get a good flow from your strokes. The bodies of the brushes are full, so it's great to use with your watercolors being very thin as well as thickened with increased pigment. The ferrule is tight on the bristles as well as the handle. The only way I see these to fail is with a lack of care and maintenance. As long as they are dried on the side and cleaned between sessions, I see no reason they would loose their texture, shape or spring. Before the first use they will need a good rinse to remove the light glue that is in place for safe transport from the manufacturer to you.

Overall, I'm very pleased with the Fuumuui Sable Hair Watercolor Brushes for their variety in size, materials used and value. I would highly recommend them to beginners and those that are well-practiced.

Thanks for reading my review. If it was helpful to you, please click the button below.

This is a great for kids that are mindful and can follow instructions. Lots of little ones are getting more education at home nowadays, so it's important to give them a break from different types of learning and let them use their hands and minds together for some tasks. The Opret Origami Kit is really cool since the colors are very vivid. We liked the dress ones the best. There are some videos to go along with it, which are helpful too.

Overall, I found this a great value for the time and enjoyment, which are very balanced. It's a really cool introduction to Oragami and fun for adults as well. It's a great family activity.

Raimarket 105 Pcs Carbon Paper Pack is great for transfering patterns to fabric and wood. I like to do embroidery projects and some wood-burning. These work great with stenciling too. I love the extras that are included because the stylus is very small, smaller than the one I have been using for scrap booking and card making. They are standard size, like printer paper. This is great for me because I can print from the printer and put the pattern straight onto a cut of fabric and get right to work. The mechanical pencils included have some refills, which is so nice and thoughtful. This is a great value. I've really had a hard time looking for some transfer paper, now I have 100 large sheets!

I appreciate you reading my review. If it was helpful, please click the button below. Have a blessed day!

This Akacraft Retro Necklace Series Embroidery Starter Kit is super cute and has everything you need, except for something to snip the threads. The booklet is very detailed. I went over it and brushed up on a few stitches and learned a couple that I've never used. I love anything miniature and this is right up my alley. I ordered Golden Beads B set, because I thought the mushroom pattern was adorable and wanted to give it a try. The hoop is a little different than what I've used before, but I like it. It has an indent in the outer hoop to hold the inner, which is great since it's a very small project. Needles, a length of chain, the jewelry findings, per-printed fabric and everything is right in the resealable bag. I'd highly recommend this to anyone that has interest in miniature embroidery projects and/or unique jewelry items. I've never seen anything like this before, so it's very exciting to do this as a project.

I have some really nice dolls in my collection, with some default outfits that are lacking in glamour. This is a perfect fabric for my dolls. It has a nice drape for small details, like tiny bust lines and skirts. I'm hand sewing these tiny dresses and it's a pleasure to hold. I inspected the fabric over a tablet back light to check for imperfections and found my cut to be flawless. The grain and weave are beautiful. This is a really nice fabric for anything from a baby's christening gown, lining, to beautiful holiday home decor. This current order is white, but I'm so pleased with it, I plan to buy a few other colors for some different doll gowns.

I hope you found my review helpful and will click the link below.

Sometimes I like to cut fabric in straight lines so I love to use a rotary cutter, but my old one was really, really small. My hands hurt often, so this is the easiest one I've held. The handle shape is good for me, even if I slip, the handle is a loop so it's much safer for me to use. It was nice to get 5 extra blades, they are oiled, so they will be in good condition when I'm ready for a replacement. The replacement blades are in their own hard plastic case, so they are stored in a safe way and will not easily dull before use.

I use a self-healing mat and a large ruler to help me cut good, straight lines. This is an excellent setup, and I went through 4 layers of cotton fabric with little effort. This could also cut paper or other materials, but I'd recommend keeping the fabric blades exclusively for cutting fabrics.

Thanks for reading my review. If it was helpful to you, please click the button below.

I had a lot of flat and angled brushes, but not much else. I'm doing a couple different types of paints like acrylic and water colors so I needed a few different types of brushes than what I already owned from painting dolls and miniatures. This is a good set all around. This is ideal for someone that has very few brushes or is looking for a little of different sizes and types. I liked that it came in it's own case and the brushes were coated with some soap or water based glue so everything was is good condition upon arrival. The sponge was creased, but that is fine as I only use them for dabbing brushes clean when I am done. I found no loose hairs or uneven cuts on the bristles. I'm mostly using the round #6 for watercolors this week, but the other brushes do their jobs well.

There are a couple things I didn't like, but this is a personal preference and not worth detracting stars. I do not like the handles being painted. From experience, sometimes those handles can chip with use and the paint chips from the handle can make a mess of a project. I would prefer handles that have no chance of dropping paint chips from the handle.

Other than the handle finish, I found this a nice well-rounded set that I will be using for several years.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful and will click the Helpful button below.

I've had a good time doing some studies with the Artunique Canvas Panels Pack of 16. I've been adding my own primer layer in Liquitex clear Gesso just to make myself feel like I'm doing something more important than I really am. Add more layers if you don't like the tooth on these panels. It's also a great way to explore some additional mediums, or just do random experiments. The nice thing about these panels is that it takes a few different types of paints well. I try to stick to acrylics, so this is working well for me. I do little studies, nothing very big. Sometimes I absolutely hate my work. Sometimes, I burn it! I would feel guilty for tossing things out that I had taken more time to prep, like stretched canvas that I've primed for 5 layers.... no guilt for tossing these when I want. I consider it practice. If I want to try something crazy, it doesn't cost much to find out if i like it or not. If I like it, I take some notes and keep it. They don't take up a lot of space, so I can simply stack them and refer to them later.

Some people may think these are difficult to mount, but I glue some ribbon in a loop on the back and that works just fine. You don't have to frame it if you don't want to.

I ordered the plain grey one because it looks like a simple bag and doesn't draw a lot of attention. I only purchase a skein, or a few, for the current project, so this holds everything I own. Before, the bag I had was suppose to be for yarn, but it was too little and had an awkward handle. I got rid of the old one and put everything into this one. I like it better. I've already had my dog blanket in the works, so I've not used the grommets to feed my yarn yet. Sometimes I get distracted when I need to unwind more length, so I'm looking forward to using those. My crochet needles, scissors, rings, and everything else fits inside the bag with room to spare. It may not hold enough yarn to make an afgan larger than throw or twin size, but it's enough for me. If you are working on a throw, sweater or anything smaller, this would work really well for all of the yarn you are using, and your tools too.

The stitching of the bag is solid and the material is thick and as durable as the fabric for most gym bags and backpacks. There are tabs to attach a longer carrying strap, but there is not one included.

I find this to be a fair to good value for the size, construction and materials used. This is a much better deal than I find in my local shops, so I'm very pleased.

I've been thinking about using some resin and/or epoxy products but I'm putting that plan off for awhile until I can find the respirator, safety equipment and setup that makes me happy.

I'm using these instead to mix some ingredients to make shower steamers at home and also for thinning glues and paints since the wood sticks absorb my essential oils and glue. They work great for this and the 'brushes' are also nice to use to get the mud out of mud mask packets. I hate getting that mud on my fingers and under my finger nails, so this makes my at-home-spa experience much more pleasant.

Most epoxy and resin products has some toxic qualities while being mixed and cured, so I've split the set and put some aside for resin use only and I'm using the other pieces for my body safe crafting.

I find the set to be an excellent value because several pieces are included and it's a versatile use product.

I wish I could say for certain what gauge of wire is used to make the chain and jump rings, but I cannot for certain. It's certainly on the thinner side and would be appropriate for earrings or other types of jewelry that would benefit from small-gauge wire.

It's nice to include the jump ring opener, but it's much too large for this gauge of wire. I would strongly suggest using two pair of smaller bent-nose pliers instead.

I've not used the chain and findings to say for certain if it truly is or is not tarnish resistant. However, it does appear for all intents and purposes to be stainless steel. It's very beautiful chain. It looks much nicer in person than in the photos. I sometimes embellish paper crafts and doll clothing, so this is certainly a good scale for those uses along with making my own custom length necklaces for different charms. It's a great value for the length of chain included and the jump rings are the appropriate gauge for the chain.

I paint dolls, mostly resin bjds. They don't wear make-up, I paint them. I can use any color of acrylic paint and now find a great matching micca powder, with the Mica Powder Pure 60 Color set. Now, they can look just like they are wearing the most glamorous make-up anyone can imagine. I was a little shocked when I opened the box, it was hard to picture in my head, WOW! There are so many! I've never seen so many different colored micca powders in one set! I don't think I will ever again need to search high and low to find just the right color for my dolls to look fabulous. I would buy this in a heartbeat after seeing them in person. The particles are all very uniform.

I'm grateful for the included spoon. That has been a struggle before, I've used a old-fashioned ink pen to scoop powder out from containers before, but that meant I had to get into my ink supply drawer every time. Now, it's so nice to have a dedicated tool just for micca powder. Photos really don't capture the beauty of these colors, so please trust me on that. They look so much more beautiful in person.

I may even try some of these colors for bath bombs I'm making for Christmas. This would be a great gift for anyone that likes to jazz up their paint, epoxy or homemade bathing products.

Thank you so much for reading my review. I hope you find it helpful.

This is a very pretty Bamboo Yarn Bowl. It has a nice, attractive design and would not look out of place on display. It's a good size and will easily accommodate most smaller skeins of yarn I use. I thought this would be nice to use instead of fighting with my skeins to unwind as I work. It was packaged well for shipping, and would be easy to wrap in the plain box to give as a gift. I wouldn't gift it as-is though.

There was one problem. The scrolled cuts and drilled holes for the yarn to exit the bowl were not finished all the way. I have to do some extra work myself to remove the splinters and sand it smooth, and bevel out the interior edges. I was surprised to find this problem, as the rest of the bowl is finished very well. The outside is polished and smooth, as is the inside of the bowl, except for the scrolled cuts and drilled holes. This was disappointing.

Aside from the unfinished holes and scroll cut, this would be a good value. Since it needs some extra effort and materials on my end, it's a fair value at best. If the shaping or exterior finishing wasn't as good as it is, I would have to give it an even lower rating. However, it's very pretty and the selection of wood was a good pick, so I give that a good deal of credit. It would have easily been a 5-star review if the interior was finished completely.

I hope the manufacturer can remedy this defect, as it's going to be a hard item to sell as it is.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

I usually use acrylic paint pot from miniature shops, or tubes of Golden or Liquitex. I'm a paint snob and usually don't mess with 'cheap paint'. I thought it would be fun to be critical of this paint set of a brand I've never heard of before. Well, it's actually very nice paint! I don't see a problem using any of your usual additives either. If you need to retard or texture your paint, this seems to take it just fine. I can't find my glossy and matte mediums, so I did not test that. I most often just thin my paints with distilled water if needed. It's smooth, not too heavy body, but not too thin either. It's great to use right out of the tube.

The set has a good array of colors. I always look for prussian blue, burnt umber and yellow ochre. All the basic colors I use most are there. Also, we have colors like pearl sapphire blue, prussian green and payne's gray that I didn't already have with my other brands. The included paper pad is a nice touch. All you need are some brushes and a container of water to wash them, then you are all set to paint!

I cannot speak on points like fading, peeling or cracking. That will require a future update. What I can say is, that out of the tube, with the selection of colors I have tested, I really can't find anything to pick on. I wanted to pick on this paint. It's a good set, and you really will have a hard time to beat the price. The only thing I can find to criticize is if this is something you really like, the individual tubes are not available as open stock. If you use up an entire tube, you will need to buy a whole set to get more. I cannot say for certain that the colors will match other brands. That's really the only gripe I can find. You will probably run out of titanium white and black before anything else.

Here are some things to keep in mind for having realistic expectations: Store your paint somewhere that keeps a consistent temperature, avoid excessive humidity and clean the cap and threaded end of the tube before storing. This will help your tubes stay clean from microbial growth. An opened tube of acrylic usually stays good for about 5 years, but up to 10 unopened depending on environmental factors. If the paint smells sour, it's had some microbial growth, so you will not get nice results. Metal tubes are a little more prone to getting pin holes, so squeeze gently, don't roll it, just press flat from the end. Acrylic is water-based, so it will clean up fine with official brush cleaner, or dish soap and water. You can swish your brush in water between colors, and it should do just fine if you have a gentle hand.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

Before, I was using a glass and paper towels but this is a much better solution for any water based painting. I primarily use acrylic paint and occasionally water colors. I like how much water is held and the two compartments, which is good for me when I'm using dark and light colors at the same time. The brush rests are handy as are the drilled holes to hold dry brushes that I'd like to have on hand for stand-by. It's big enough to hold an ample amount of water, but not so big that it takes up a lot of space on my work surface.

It's a good value for the size and the lid is nice. When I transport the soiled water to another room, I don't risk sloshing it out onto the carpet.

Thanks so much for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

I had been looking for some chain to make an elaborate outfit for a doll. I wanted to use chain between eyelets and jump rings to attach a collar to the bodice of a dress. This will work perfectly and the type of metal used is so common that I am not worried it will match well with any aluminum silver colored jump rings or eyelets. The 3.5mm measurement is from the side to side of each link, the length of each link is 6mm. This is a perfect size for so many projects. It will only take a little for me per project, so this should last me a long time. For those that make necklaces and other jewelry, you will certainly have a good supply on hand for several projects. That makes for a great value. I appreciate it was wrapped on a card and placed inside a resealable bag. It makes storage easy and I'm not worried it may tangle while in my crafting box.
