I love that there are 36 different sheets with several templates on each one. My favorite ones are sheets 17 and 18, but I'm happy with all of them. These are a good thickness. I can easily trace these onto wood for wood-burning, air-brush over them with paint or use my embossing tool to do a variety of paper crafts. They are thick enough for repeated use, but not so thick that I fear my embossing tool perforating some of my thinner papers. I'm glad the plastic is sturdy enough for me to wash them when I get pencil marks or paint on them. I'm currently using the moon and star from sheet 10 to make a wooden Christmas ornament. It's going well! I plan to use some of the patterns to paint tiny designs on tiny shirts for dolls. They only arrived today and I'm thrilled and getting started with the ornament first.

For a very long time, I've been looking for a light box to make my own transfers for embroidery. I hesitated, because the ones I did manage to find in the craft stores were very expensive compared to the amount of time I would need to use them. Instead, I have been waiting for very bright days and making my transfers on the window when the sun is bright by taping my papers onto the glass. This worked, but it was a hassle, especially since I live and work at night.

The A4 LED Light Box is a great solution for this. I really appreciate the magnetic surface. Two small magnets are included, and were strong enough to hold both paper pieces into place. This is very light weight, and very thin, so it's easy to store and move around while in use. It takes a micro-USB cord, which is included. The light box is very light weight and does not have an internal battery. If you would like to use the light box on-the-go, a power bank is a must. Otherwise, I don't find it difficult to use the box while it's connected to a power supply. The total lit surface measures 32cm (12.5 inches) by 21.5cm (8.4 inches). There are marked measurements on the top and side of the lit surface for 30cm and 21cm. There are three brightness levels from the LED lights. For my transfers, the lowest setting was sufficient, but the brightest setting did not hurt my sensitive eyes.

The only potential downside to the light box may be the textured surface. It was not a problem for me, but if you need a very smooth surface, this may be an issue for you. It may be possible to add a transparent sheet of film to remedy this, but I have no need and could not say for sure. I cannot speak to the durability, but if/when it does cease to function, I will make sure to update this review. This may be a very fragile electronic, so I would not recommend this be purchased for children.

This is very economical, and would be good for hobbyists, students and artists. It would help light up the surface for many different projects from origami, stenciling, tracing, bead-work, or serve as a back light for reading. It would make a nice gift, and would be easy to wrap in paper.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

HASHI Non Toxic Long Soft Pastel Set for Professionals has an impressive amount of pigment and a decent texture. Compared to brands that are more familiar to me like Unison, Rembrandt and Schmincke, I would judge that these are medium-soft pastels. Particles are not falling off as much as some of the very loose chalk-y pastels I have used, but they are not so firm that they need a lot of pressure for the particles to transfer to another material. I've used some terrible pastels in the past that were much too firm with too-little pigment at a price much higher than these. They are a little buttery, but not as much as say, Schminke, which are the softest I own. They are much softer than Rembrandt. Sometimes, it's better to use a hand-rolled pastel, and other times, mass-produced and formed pastels are best. It really depends on the project and artist. It's good to have a variety and this is a good set for beginners or a nice addition to a hobbyist or artist's collection.

Although I have a pretty good collection of pastels already, HASHI Soft Pastels are a nice addition. The colors are very vibrant and the pigments are not at all muted. These are all strong, bold colors. The white is white like clouds and the black is black as soot from burnt wood. I most often use chalk pastels to customize blank dolls made from resin, vinyl or ABS plastic. These add some different colors to my collection I do not have, and will be suitable to use with dry brushing.

My only tip for other customers is to have some rubber bands or string handy, as the box's lid does not fit very snugly and if you want to take your box along for travel, you will need to secure the box closed.

The overall quality of the pastels is good and the price is very economical. I count this set as a good value, and will recommend it to my doll-customizing hobby friends.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you found it helpful.

This is the most complete set I have ever seen. I previously purchased a Big Name kit at the same price that only included 3 crochet needles. I'm so happy to have a nice, complete set of needles and all the accessories I could need. All you need is to choose yourself some yarn and you are ready to go! I tried to find some row counters locally, and have been unsuccessful. I finally have the row counters, along with several color-coded locking stitch markers, more thread needles than I will ever really need and a ruler. I've always wanted a tiny measuring tape that had the retracting mechanism, so I'm really very thrilled with the kit. The fact that it came with a case is a huge bonus. The carrying case that is nice for travel and there's a little room to spare for anything else you may wish to add. I didn't have a carrying case before now, so it's much appreciated. This kit really does have all you need to keep occupied on-the-go. I like the rubber handles, sometimes my hands do get tired and these give me a lot more time to make stitches before fatigue in my hands sets in. The ink on the handles may rub away with use, so I'm going to mark them myself on the end of the handle with a permanent marker. That's really the only thing I have found that could use improvement, but it's a very minor point.

Overall, this is a great little kit. I wish I had come across this before I bought my other kit. This would make a really nice gift for yourself or anyone you know that might have interest in crochet.

Thank you for reading my review. I hope you have found it helpful.

Finally, Velcro has made a very thin version, VELCRO Brand Sleek and Thin for Fabrics ( 6ft x 3/4in) Tape, White . It's also currently available in black, which I will be ordering very soon. I occasionally make little clothes for dolls and other toys. It's very frustrating to try to use any other hook and loop fastener. Most are much too think, and I've only seen fasteners of appropriate profiles on commercially made doll clothes but could not find a source locally for making my own, until now.

I cut the Velcro strip to make it more narrow for a very small doll dress, then I did 'mean' things to it such as try to stretch it out, rub it around on various objects and such. It showed very minimal fraying, so this would be ideal for using for anything meant for children. I would not anticipate any fraying once it's cut evenly and stitched into place. Also, the hook side is not at all rough or scratchy. I would not hesitate to use this for clothing or toys for very, very small children or on delicate fabrics that the higher profile tape may snag.

I'm very glad Velcro has now made a very thin and gentle fastener. It's a good value for the 6 foot long tape, and is packaged well for storing remaining tape between projects. I will certainly be recommending this products to other friends that sew for dolls and children, as it's a product we have been wishing for as long as I can recall.

Nice, basic set of sewing feet. they fit on my Singer sewing machine just fine. They are easy to take on and off. I do recommend these.

I love these scissors. They are sharp and get the job done. But, they are also gorgeous! I really appreciate a pair of sharp scissors. There is nothing worse than scissors that won't cut properly, leaving frayed ends of thread or worse, the thread just gets trapped between the blades without cutting. You won't have that problem with either of these. The bonus is that these are just so pretty. It's a small thing, but it just feels nice and fancy to use them. I highly recommend them.

This rotary cutter is fantastic! It is really sharp, and so easy to use. Make sure you have a proper cutting board under your fabric or you will ruin your table. The blades are easy to change, just be careful, because they are REALLY sharp. Making the bias tape takes a little practice, but the tools provided make it fairly simple. I highly recommend.
This is beautiful cross-stitch fabric. It is suitable for smaller work. I am very happy with it and highly recommend this economical pack.

I really like this nifty little tape measure. I have always like tape measures that are retractable and tuck away neatly in a little case. It's just a pet peeve of mine that loose tape measures have a way of getting tangled up on things and lost easily. When they are in a case like this, it is just more tidy and easier to find. The retracting mechanism works great, zipping the tape back into the case at the touch of a button. I highly recommend.

This set of brushes is really nice. The brushes are very fine-tipped, easy to hold and make a nice fine line with your paint. I like the pretty case they come in. It keeps the tips from getting squashed during transport and storage. I recommend these for yourself or as a gift for the art lover you know.

I really like this kit. It comes with everything you need to make a simple plant hanger. I especially like the detailed explanations and diagrams of the knots and how to make them. The instructions are easy to follow, and the skill you learn will be useful beyond the scope of this project. I plan on giving this to my daughter-in-law for Christmas. She's recently taken an interest in macrame, and I think she will love this. I highly recommend this kit, especially for beginners.

There are lots of things to like about these handy little thread snips. First of all, I love how they fold up, hiding the point. That makes it easier to carry in your pocket or purse without poking a hole in the corner...or getting jabbed by the point when fishing around for it in a sewing bag or your purse. The mechanism for hiding the point is easy to do. Just open fully and push the handles. The points slide easily inside the handles and...no more points.

As a cutter, they are sharp. I used them to cut the notches on a pattern. They cut the cotton fabric easily, making nice sharp notches. They are also fairly comfortable in the hand. I recommend these thread snips very highly.

Easy to follow instructions. I made this apron for my daughter-in-law for Christmas. I like all the options in design that this pattern gives you. The instructions were easy to follow and the apron came out fantastic. My daughter-in-law LOVED it! I made the one with the bib and cute pockets on the front of the full skirt. Very cute!

Where has this been all my life?! I really love this bead board. The nifty notebook covering keeps the board from cracking, which is what happened to my last board. The straps and pocket on the inside are really handy too, for organizing things.

My daughter got into making her own jewelery and as such, requires a lot of materials from glass beads to wires, to elastics to all of the hardware for bracelets and earrings.

The Yochus 21600pcs Glass Seed Beads is a large set of glass beads that are quite small and comes with many pieces of hardware that will help buyers get started with making little charms, pendants and bracelets. Add these beads to other irregular stones and other hardware to start creating rings and earrings.

With a wide range of colors to choose from, this set supplements my daughter's quickly growing collection of jewelry hardware that allows her to express her creativity in various ways.

There really isn't much to complain about. Whether one gets good use out of this set really is up to the imagination.

The rings and clasps are not intended for making jewelry that will touch the skin so there really isn't a concern as to whether they are hypoallergenic.

The ifergoo 92 Colors Polymer Clay Starter Kit is a great starter kit for anyone who wishes to make little clay models. In the case of my daughter, she is using it to make small jewelry such as pendants and earrings.

The kit comes with many clay-working tools and a great variety of colors. Of course, the colors can be further customized as polymer clay can be mixed with each other to create new colors. I imagine it takes a lot of experience to figure out how much of each color to add.

My daughter found that the clay was easy to work with and doesn't have an oily residue. It tends to be a little bit sticky, though. She highly recommends this kit.

The Bearly Art Wash Tape Set that I got are the "Macaron Mix". They are a set of pastel-colored tape with no additional decorations and they are exactly as you'd expect. The pastel colors work well to bring lively color to a plain page. They stick well and have a matte surface. The rolls are fairly large so they will last a while.

They are exactly what my daughter wanted for her bullet journal.

UV resin is so easy to use and way safer fume-wise than traditional resin. There’s no pesky mixing required, just pour, spread and cure.

No yellowing. Crystal clear coverage.

Resin has always overwhelmed me. The thought of mixing two steps together equally always gives me a bit of anxiety haha. Then there’s the dry time. Yikes.

UV resins gave me the option to try my hand at something I’ve been wanting to do and I absolutely love it!

It’s easy to use. Hardens quick under a UV light and has little to no smell. Even when curing.

This is perfect for handmade items like keychains, jewelry and bookmarks.

I’ve had so much fun making stuff with this.

Highly recommend!

I absolutely love the tree of life symbol. I’ve always been drawn to it and try to incorporate it in any way with my jewelry or home decor.

The tree of life symbolizes wealth, wisdom and knowledge. When you wear it, it promotes beauty, health, good luck, and healing.

These pendants showcase gorgeous pieces of tumbled stone in the wire work of the tree.

The stones included are:

Clear Crystal, Amethyst, Lapis Lazuli, Green Aventurine, Citrine, Red Agate and Red Stone.

These stones have different metaphysical healing properties that benefit the wearer in several ways.

Clear Crystal/Quartz is a purifier and booster of the properties of other crystals.

Amethyst is a grounding stone which is perfect for anxiety. Lapis Lazuli is great for stress and bring peace to the wearer. Green Adventurine is a healing stone and aids in releasing negative energy. Citrine is the heated form of amethyst and is great for clarity and creativity. Red Agate is a protection stone which protects the wearer from negative energy and helps with anxiety.

These pendants are beautiful made with silver wire which is wrapped perfectly with care.

I ordered this set (airbrush newbie) assuming one of these would allow my airbrush quick connect to connect to my 1/8 inch airhose.  NOPE.  If you are in the same boat, you will need to order something else - probably a male + female 1/8" (or 1/4 depending on your airbrush) quick connect set.  I guess it's my fault for not reading the description clearly, but this set really should have included a female 1/8 quick connect, since that is the key piece missing to connect most new airbrushes to the average air hose.

Everyone knows  Vallejo makes great primers and paints.  However, white is the most popular color and seems to be almost always out of stock.  The Mecha primers bind very well and are strong, but they also come out of the bottle thin.  You will need an airbrush if you want an even-looking primer coat.  You can apply this with a brush, too, but the coat will NOT look even, so you will need to base coat with something very opaque.  Anyway it's a great primer.  I'd recommend white/ivory or something light, as its easier to darken than lighten with paint.

First, the good things...The airbrush produces a fairly smooth finish, providing you thin your paint well. Also, the airbrush is fairly quiet - the noise certainly isn't enough to bother your neighbors or people in the next room. The handle and airbrush exterior have a glossy finish, and there is a 4-stage battery meter on the bottom of the handle next to the USB-c charging port. Also, you can adjust the amount of paint sprayed using the screw at the back, which controls how far the trigger can be pulled.

Now, the bad...the max pressure is around 15 psi, and the pressure CANNOT be adjusted. Also, the spray pattern cannot be adjusted either. That means you can't spray really wide for painting large areas, and you also can't spray thin narrow lines for detail work. I can't believe this is advertised for nail art, as i SERIOUSLY doubt anyone using this airbrush could paint their nails better than my 4-year old can paint hers. Being made in China, I have serious doubts about how long this will continue to function, but so far it's worked fine.

So while small detail work is out of the question, what this CAN do is give you a cheap, low-noise way of applying primer and simple base coats on miniatures or small objects. If that's what you're after, you'll be happy. If you try to make this do something else, you will be disappointed.

My daughter (almost 5 years old) loves this and we have already spent several hours sculpting various things.  Age 5 is probably a bit young for this considering the small pieces and clay hardness.  Polymer clay can be fairly hard initially until it warms up, so it may be a challenge for little fingers/hands.  This set is probably best for kids just a tad bit older.  The set includes every color of clay you need (the clay blocks are small, though) and they are all individually wrapped.  The set also includes plastic sculpting tools, and some decorative sparkles / jewelry things that my daughter really loves.  It also has a little booklet with a few sample designs to get them started.  It really is a lot of stuff for the price, so I would give this 5 stars.  Just be careful not to give this to children who are too young.
This works perfectly to remove a glossy finish from painted areas that you do not want to be glossy.  It looks milky straight out of the bottle, but dries clear.

Do not panic when this paint arrives at your house and looks MUCH darker than the display photo! The display photo shows a light brown color (to simulate a thin single coat) but the paint pot is actually a VERY dark brown. Wait! Try it out and you'll see the paint comes out much lighter (thin coat) than the color in the pot. I almost made the mistake of requesting a return upon seeing the dark brown paint pot, but it IS actually correct. It's a wonderful paint, imo, and probably the best among GW contrast paints.

For anyone who has never used GW Citadel contrast paints, they work very differently from most regular acrylic paints. This will NOT replace a brown paint of the same shade, but is very useful in other ways. Contrast paints are MUCH thinner than typical acrylic paint, like a mix of a paint and a wash, but with extra pigment added. Just one thin layer will produce a vibrant, slightly translucent color. Applying a bit more will darken cracks and folds like a wash will. It can be difficult to produce a uniformly even color over large areas with contrast paints. So if you were painting a large, smooth cloak on a 100mm model this would be a poor choice. But where these contrast paints really shine is painting smaller, detailed areas. They can also save time for batch painting, if you don't wanna spend hours highlighting/shading, washing,etc. big groups of minis, but you still want them to look good. For example, if you like painting armies of stuff like Warhammer, contrast paints would be perfect since they add a bit of detail/contrast like washes do.

After doing some research on contrast paints, I found that this particular color (Snakebite Leather) is considered by many painters to be one of the best and most useful among all contrast paints. After using it, I can see why. The unique nature of GW contrast paints really does simulate leather well. Want a darker leather? Just apply another coat. I would recommend buying this paint FIRST if you are considering getting into contrast paints, as this color is probably the most useful among them all. Think about how common brown is on miniatures, right? You can produce several shades of brown (with a leather effect) with this paint by applying multiple coats. Unlike some of my other contrast paints, I WILL buy this one again when it runs out as it's extremely useful. If you don't have this, give it a try. You may have to wait a bit for shipping, as it's often out of stock due to popularity.
Great quality; I ordered a 2nd pack from same company after I was so happy with these.
Love how this turned out. It is helpful to watch the YouTube video as well. Love the results
Very easy to apply, dries fast and is easy to clean. I noticed some of the color comes off in my rags when I clean but it’s not a big thing. Overall, the color in the table and chairs remains.
I use these to make "eyes" in polymer clay sculptures by painting the flat side.  These are well made,  clear,  without imperfections.  They did not melt at 270F while in my sculpture.

This is the perfect clay for kids (and adult hobbists!) It’s the most stretchy, soft and light clay I ever tried! It will dry very quickly, which can be great because it will then have sturdy structure so you can add to it. The inside of the figure might still be a bit damp, but the outside firms up within minutes so you can hold it, and continue to add/subtract from it.

If you’re working on a piece of the clay (stretching and shaping it) and it gets too dry, add a tiny bit of water and knead it and it will come back to life). Keep extra clay in the tubs or if you’ve mixed a custom color, put it in some plastic wrap or in a small covered glass jar, so it will stay moist until use.

The final piece is super light but really firm (no wiggle or warp…not flexible when dry) and dense (good for some cosplay pieces?). It can then be carved, drilled, sanded, and you can add fresh clay by wetting the dry area and adding new clay. Only disadvantage might be that if you want a keepsake item, some kind of sealer is needed, since the final piece will not be waterproof.

Love it! The brochure says it’s non-toxic…gluten-free…no pthalates…

Perfect! Better than pic.
I never thought I’d like metal knitting needles as I’ve always hated the ones I’ve tried before. But these are really nice.

Seems to be just what I need. I'm not certain how durable the paper is yet, as I have not used it. I plan to update this review after I give it a try. 🙂 I did like the value for my money, because it is a very large roll of paper. Should last us quite a while.
Quality isn't great.
My daughter loves them and had a lot of fun making crafts with them.
This set has colors that really pop.  We love it.
Very pretty colors. But the markers leaked/exploded. It was 30 degrees outside, but packed with several other items. Haven’t tried to use them on crafts yet, I hope there’s ink left.
It's pretty watery paint, bot good for crafting. I had to use about 4 or 5 coats on primed wood to make it not super streaky. May be fine for painting on paper.
Great for decoration
Strong and sturdy. A must have
Granddaughter loved it
I use this stuff all the time but I need to remember to put a plate down underneath the tube.  The glue oozes out even when you are not squeezing the tube.
Not suitable for the kind of delicate work I use them for but I'll find something to do with them
Gold bales turn black with wear
I was planning to use these on a paper project. They are pretty heavy because they actually are real glass. Perfectly cut squares. Very flashy.

Lots of ribbon for sure. I'm not fond of the Styrofoam core it is wrapped on. It's loosely wrapped and as soon as you handle it the whole thing falls off the core. The ribbon also seems to want to fray very easily.

I have used foil before in clay work. This is about the same as far as using it goes. Be sure you have no drafts in the vicinity or this will blow all over the place before you can even get it on your food. Very fragile and difficult, but worth it if you can make it work. Extra bling for sure!

I like these strips rhinestones for embellishment on my greeting cards. This is a nice selection of colors to have in my stash. The black and the dark green colors are not at all sparkly. The black especially is kind of a matte finish. Bit disappointing.

I have been gathering up as much of this mosaic glass as I can. It's so much fun to just sit down and start to work on the project. I taught and did stained glass for 25 years. Mosaic work is fun without all the soldering and lead. Now if I want to do something small, I don't have to drag out the whole glass studio to cut a few pieces. Good color selection of the basic colors. Nothing exotic. Good for multicolor pieces. If you want to  do something large, that only requires certain colors, you will have to sort through the pieces and buy many tiles. Different shapes included.

As soon as I saw this product, I knew I could use it for the abundance of washi tape I have accumulated. My joy ended there. The instructions to put this together as well as the way it is engineered are flawed. Grab yourself a hammer and follow me kids!

First off, unpack the pieces and throw the instructions in the bin. Mark the "A" holes on the uprights with a marker or sharpie. Then take your hammer and tap in all 70 of the little posts. They don't go in by hand and it would be easier to do this in the beginning then at the end like the instructions state.

Take your cross pieces and your 'B' screws and fasten them in if they will go (the angle is weird and the bottom row doesn't fit in the notch at all). As you work your way from the bottom to the top, be aware of those holes you marked and go ahead and put the back stand pieces on. This is where it's tricky. There are 2 thickness of acrylic to go through. The edges aren't beveled, so the screws don't fit flush. A couple of the "B" screws weren't long enough to fasten through the stand piece, the upright and the crossbar. I had to go to my hardware stash and find something longer. Swearing is appropriate at this point.

Work your way up adding the crosspieces and fastening the stand pieces as you go. If you do them first they are in the way of putting the cross pieces on.

While this works, I always delete stars when I have to rewrite the instructions and provide my own hardware to get the job done.

I basically needed the cutters from this set because my old ones were worn out and had divots all along the blades. These cut well. The edges meet up perfectly. The handles aren't too stiff to manipulate.
I got these to use for mixed media purposes. They are a little too rough for a painting application.
These at least have clear graphics and are consistently shaped.  I hope to figure out a way to incorporate them into my greeting cards. Lots of variety of themes which are really fun.

I kind of remember reading in the description that this is stainless steel chain. The only reason I got it. However, the packaging is totally lacking in any information about the materials used. For this reason I can't use it because I don't know what it is. The chain itself seems well made, but if it is just base metal, I need to know that for hypoallergenic reasons.
These guys are sturdy, and bristly and very good at cleaning and getting into the nooks and crannies. Best pipe cleaners I have seen in a long time.

I have used palettes like these for a long time. They are good for painting parties and the like. Pretty easy to clean, although they do stain over time. If paint dries in them, it can usually be peeled out when dry.

This may be a wonderful product, but without any instructions on use anywhere on the product or the packaging, I have no idea where to begin. I am so over having to get online to get instructions for review items. My art studio is on a remote part of my property and there is no internet access. Not all of us live in a bustling metropolis.
These are pretty beads, but they aren't "natural". They look like they are chips held together with epoxy. One or two have some natural matrix in them, but they don't look real.

I currently have quite a collection of wax seals. This one is by far the most sensational for detail and quality. It is a bit larger than other, so to get a good impression you will need 4 wax pellets. This has quite a deep relief on the bee part and a larger diameter with great detail. It is positively stunning when it is used!
Love this paper for small collage and decoupage projects. Nice color selection and you can just sit down and get to work with a minimum amount of preparation.

I really like this product. Very versatile for storage. Two deeper drawers and one that is more shallow for brush storage. I took the dividers out of the drawers for by purpose because it allowed me to store more tubes of paint. Exceptionally well made and very attractive. Stacking several of these in a studio would certainly solve the space problem.

I use a lot of these kinds of gems for bling on greeting cards.  These stick very well. Lots of shapes and sizes make for a good variety with lots of possibilities. I even had a few half pearl shapes included in my stash.
I find these boards to be very well cut and sanded. Consistent shapes and very smooth for painting or vinyl.  I good size for medium sized projects too.

I really like that these are on individual sheets by color. It makes it very easy to work with. 3 different sizes of each color. Good selection of colors. Not very sparkly like some of these rhinestones, but good enough for my purposes. I would say they don't have great adhesion. I was able to pick them off of a paper card I put them on without any damage to the paper.

I was hoping to use these in a project, but I probably won't. The graphics are not sharp and kind of out of focus. Nice variety of people and shapes, but they could have been executed with more quality. They seem to stick really well.

This didn't work very permanently in my testing and use. It applies easily, but even after a few hours you can still separate your paper without damage. I applied it, burnished it and let it set for several hours and never did get what I consider a "permanent" bond.
For some reason this color selection of charms is much better than the white metal selection. Larger charms. Better variety. Multiples. Good quality.

Disappointed because the charms are very small and there aren't duplicates of any.  The larger pieces might work for some of my projects and the quality seems OK, but overall, I've gotten better charms in the past.

I've been playing around with this for a couple of days. The dyes are really not very vibrant or strong. Did a couple of projects and after they are dry they are pretty much pastel. It also seems to wash out on the first rinse after drying. Not much dye in the package either. Could be fun I suppose for kids. Wouldn't really recommend it.
Smooth finished paper cardstock in a variety of lovely colors. Very useful for the greeting card maker. Fits in the cutting machine just fine. A nice selection all in one.

I got these to decorate in various ways for make up bags or junk bags in a purse. The zippers are good enough and they seem well constructed. The canvas is pretty thin. They will work for my purposes.

A nice color palette. Some of the darker colors could be a little more muted.  The first thing I noticed though, is a vast inconsistency in the softness, flexibility and texture of each different color. Some are supple and flowing. Most are stiff and crease when you bend or fold them. I guess it depends on what you want to use this for as to whether it will work for you.

I make greeting cards and note cards. I plan to use these strung together for lettering. There are lots of each letter in the box and they are the perfect size for my projects. All uniform and easy to read.
This set has a good color range. They came well packaged and none were broken which is a plus.  They seem a little hard, but do blend with pressure. I mostly use mine to make chalk marbled paper.

I had a project in mind for these, but the quality far exceeds my expectations. I may have to do something a little more spectacular with these.  Great variety. The bails are very sturdy.  Really nice!
This is a lovely book with good quality graphics and very sturdy paper. Lovely artwork and a good selection of themes to color.
These don't really scream galactic to me. They are just kind of mottled and sort of plain. Standard width. Good for cards and sealing envelopes. 7 rolls seems odd as well. Why not 6 or 8?

These are nice, smooth wooden eggs with a small flat area for a base. I like them because the base area doesn't not detract from the shape of the egg. The eggs still stand up, but the shape at the bottom remains more intact than others I have. Good size and I can't wait to start drawing on these.

Finally paint markers that have small enough tips to use for detail.  These have good color selection and they have no odor. They weren't hard to get the paint to flow and have a nice, firm, pointy nib. A win if you do detailed art on rocks or write text.

As usual there are no use guidelines. I tried this in just clear resin and it seems to work well. I also added some to resin that had mica powder mixed into it already and that also worked. UV light activates it pretty quickly but it doesn't seem to last long.
I don't think this is as good as the Versamark variety, but it did work OK.

I really like these. Fine tip paint markers are iffy at best. These really are fine point. The paint flows well and is dark and black, not watery. I do a lot of line work on rocks and over paint pours, etc.  These are perfect.

This is a better kit then the one I originally started out with. It has more professional tips.  The instructions are at least good enough to get your started. It would be nice if they included examples of what each tip can do as I don't have the patience to connect them all and do a test. Getting the tips in requires a lot of screwing and unscrewing of tiny screws. They directions say to set the heat at 400. This isn't calibrated well enough to pick a specific temperature. I never could get it to zero in on any one setting. I like the fact that you can plug in 2 handles at once and that there are stands to put them in while you are using them.  If I knew what effect each tip was supposed to do I could give this more stars.

I have used many brush cleaners and they all have different directions. Dilute? Don't? Soak? Not a single instruction to use on the entire label. Tried it diluted a bit like my other cleaners and it really wasn't that great. I wish manufacturers would spend less time on labels telling me how good their product is and more time on telling me how to use it. That's the only way to get a good review.

A really good selection of bits for a mixed media artist. The stickers are made from a vellum like paper so you can put them on a text piece and be able to sort of see through them. There is a large piece of faux newsprint. A packet with a bunch of really nice genuine postage stamps. Washi tape. A piece of lace. Lots of paper pieces to cut out or tear. Really good kit.

I really love these. I like they don't have a pre drilled hole so you can use them in whatever way you want. Nice thickness, not flimsy like some I have gotten.  Smooth and easy to paint. Lots of possibilities for uses.
Not impressive. Easy to use for sure except to get the bottles open. Looked good when it was wet, but the shimmer and sparkle diminished when dried.

These are so soft and make blending ink very easy. I've never had anything that worked quite this well. Just whisk across your ink pad and apply to the paper and blend. I deducted a star because I don't quite understand why they have colored bristles to start with. It makes it hard to tell how much ink you have on there and also when they need to be cleaned.

I wanted this mold to make a display for my products in my Etsy shop. I did not pour it in resin but poured it in plaster. It takes about 3 cups of material to fill this mold and I just didn't want to spend that much resin. The plaster worked well and it unmolded with ease. Easy to see through the mold to the bubbles so that you can correct them. Very detailed item.

There are no instructions or guidelines with this so be prepared to watch some videos to learn how to use it. Having said that the videos I could find used a much better, higher end tool than this. I wasn't impressed. I employed the same techniques as the better tool and really got a fair amount of pilling on my felting. Not easy to use. It did gather my test piece together enough to turn it over, but from there I went back to using my hands and rolling.
I have several ideas for these using mixed media art. Ornaments for my seasonal tree for one. Good consistent size and shape.
I paint a lot of rocks and canvases and gesso is a must have for every artist. This brand works fine for me.

I was very excited to get this. I use a lot of it to back paintings for a finished look. This is kind of thin. It has no body. Fine for wrapping but not for backing or anything that requires a bit of heft.
These are smooth and easy to paint on for kids and adults alike. They can be made as simply or as detailed as you like.  Good value!

Believe or not some of thee arrived broken. They were in a crappy box that was shoved in a plastic envelope. The weight of the rocks opened the box, tore the mesh bag and the envelope apparently was dropped. Several rocks were broken in half. Aside from that they aren't very uniform rocks for the value listed. Even the ones not damaged are not very good for painting on.

I have several sets of these stamps. This one is very nice and I especially like the bee. I would love to get something a selection that didn't include a tree of life stamp. Have 5 or so of them now. Could we mix it up a little and take that one out of the rotation?? It takes about 3 wax pellets to get a perfect stamp with this size.

I really wish these were made a bit differently. They are hard to get on and off. You have to stretch them way out to get them over even a small hand. I hate that everything has to be put on them with jump rings which is never a secure way to put things on a bracelet. Would love to be able to thread some large hole beads on these for some color and interest, but the only way to do that is with head pins and loops.

How in the world does a product like this ever get sold in the US? They should pay people to buy it instead of the other way around. I wanted something to hold an excess of craft paint bottles. Instead I got an airplane model assembly piece that took and hour and half to put together and ended up with broken pieces.
The assembly directions are ludicrous. If you have to take it apart because something isn't right, well you are just out of luck. That's when the damage occurs,. This is made from very thin wood that has been laser cut. Some of the piece are incompletely cut so as to make them unable to be removed from the background. There are a few extra pin pieces so when you break them you can adjust.
Once this is together, it is fairly useless. The drawers have dividers, but not enough pieces to complete both drawers. The shelf in the front is flimsy and the holes for the paint brushes at the top (I assume that's what they are) are too small to be useful.
As for holding paint, if you loaded this with semi full bottles of paint it would collapse under the weight of said paint. I only tried a few bottles which were enough to see that they don't fit in the holes deep enough and the whole things is just fiddly, and very poorly engineered.

Where do I submit my bill for time wasted on this crap?
Useful for doing embellishment on poured canvases. Easy to lay on and get the outline or shape and them fill in with your own style.

I've been wanting to get some of these for a long time now. Just to try for pouring. They are a nice size. The gesso finish seems good. I think they are rather crudely stapled on the back and will need further finishing to hang.

I've been reading about some of the off brand carving blocks and thought I might try this out for some carving practice before I get into my Soft Kut sheet.  This includes several colors of 6 x 5 sheets of rubber. White core and color on either side.  I found it harder to carve than brand names. The included tools for the most part look more like wood carving tools. There are a couple that I could use, but most of them were totally foreign as to what they would do.  I haven't finished my block, but I'm not expecting great results in the printing department with this material.  Best to stick to  tried and true materials for this art. It takes a lot of time to carve a good block and you don't waste that time on inferior supplies.

I finally got a chance to mix up these chemicals and try this out. It is a fascinating process.  3 stars because it would be so very helpful to have some specific ideas and directions. Everything seemed kind of vague to me. I supposes part of the allure is playing with it and figuring it out as you go, but frankly I don't have the time for that or the patience. Have had mixed results on paper and haven't tried it on anything else until that gets better.

Been wanting to try some of these fancy looking little scissors. The small set was way too uncomfortable for me to use with my arthritic joints. The holes are smallish and the design of the metal pushes in on your fingers in an uncomfortable way. The larger pair was easier to use. In the final analysis they are just scissors. Pretty but not all that functional.
I got these for using in needle felting animals. They vary too much I think for a jewelry purpose. Kind of have to sort through a bunch of each color to find 2 that are perfectly matched.
These are very thin and perfect for a laser cutter. Now if only I could get mine to work...

If you are a beginner this might be worth your time.

Everything included.
Paint is very thin and watery. Not very saturated or opaque.
Low odor is relative. Doesn't smell as bad as the outdoor paint from this manufacturer, but smells more than any other brand I've used.
Included are some various canvas boards which every paint pour person knows don't work. They warp.
Same with the thin wooden pieces. They warp when they dry.
Would have been better to send a few stretched canvases and skip the pieces doomed to fail.
When dry this paint has a very high gloss finish and the color saturation is weak.

This gets 5 stars for the fact that there are legible, easy to read instructions as there should always be when you are selling a "kit".  Everything you need to learn this. Lots of good diagrams and text to learn the complicated knots and make really cool things. Beads and rings and a bill old ball of twine. What fun!

I used some of these today on a faux leather bracelet project. They are pretty easy to put on once you sort out the directions. A little hard to snap shut, but I guess that is better than being too lose. Good color selection and great possibilities.
I tried these on several things. Best on paper. Large nib. Good colors. Fun to use.

I basically got this for the brayer that is in it. It's nice to roll over the drills to keep them in place and get the best adhesion. Extra trays are always welcome. I will say I'm not that excited about the storage containers. They are kind of like a tic tac box with a narrow opening and a flip top lid.  They are hard to fill first off. I only did a couple to try it and I won't be using them.

Works as well as any other gouache I have. Nothing exceptional about it. I do like the larger tubes in this set. I have trouble with being able to use what is leftover and dried on  my palette. I can never get any of the brands to rehydrate properly and be reusable. So bigger tubes to use fresh paint every time is a plus.

Kind of in between 3 and 4 stars on this. These are small and very thin. Nice sized holes. They remind me of little washers for on bolts they are that thin. Bright colors. Nothing subtle. Good for teenagers.

it takes 2 men and a gorilla to unmold this. The other issue is the way it is designed on the bottom there is no way to  get the bubbles out under the lip of the mold. I would only recommend this if you have a strong man to help get it unmolded.
Enjoying using this resin. It has a good consistency. Sets fast and gets hard. I made a gorgeous pendant in a bezel with some dried flowers in about 5 minutes. Instant gratification.

This item is packaged all together. In other words you get a plastic container with the beads and everything else all dumped in together and the instructions I guess are in the bottom of the container.  Just to get all the parts out makes a mess of beads going everywhere. Surely there has to be a better way. I can't imagine too many parents that want to clean up the mess this makes just to open the box.
Nice artwork to color in. The paper is a bit thinner than other coloring books and smoother in texture. Be sure to  put something between the pages or take them out because marker bleeds through.
These molds are no the typical molds I've been getting. Far finer detail and a much thicker sturdier mold. The finished product for these will be worthy of note. Far superior to anything else.

I've been getting a lot of canvases lately from all kinds of sources. Unfortunately these fall at the low end of the quality range. The canvas isn't primed well and not stretched well at all. Some of the canvases in my pack have ripples coming into the corners. I like the fact that they miter the canvas on the backside at the corners so at least it isn't overlapping and sticking out at the edges.  They are just too thin and loose for most work.

I was skeptical about this mold as far as hanging glasses on it. Normally these are a little too thin and no matter what resin you use the finished product can be a little wimpy. However this mold is thicker than the normal geode mold.  I am at a loss as to the correlation of the leaf molds with the wine glass mold, but they are thicker quality molds as well.
This is going to be fun. Haven't done one of these in awhile. Lots of yarn and the packs are numbered so you know what color goes where.  I'll be looking for more of these.

Very impressed with this set of paints and extras. I've only starting using gouache about 6 months ago and I really like it. The perfect combination of acrylic and watercolor.  This set has very nice brushes, a palette, sponge and more all included. Everything you need at hand to paint. What a great gift for an artist, old or young.
Can't wait to pour this mold!! Awesome details on each key.  A hole premade in each one for stringing. There are a million possibilities for these. Amazing!
I do a lot of art. These are great for the back of canvases to string the hanging wire through. Easy to use. Good quality.  Nice box to store.

Nice pens. Kind of hard to get the paint to flow. By the time you get it to the tip you have mashed the already extra large nib even larger. Why can't they make these with a finer nib and a better dispensing system?

I gave this 4 stars for being made in the USA.  Otherwise I think it is kind of pricey for a thin piece of laminated wood. Mine seemed to be just a little warped too. I think if you are making signs for personal use you could use some of these. If you do artwork to sell this is prohibitive for the size and quality.

So far I am liking this. The foam is good quality. However the paper that you place on top of it could be thicker I think. I was mixing kind of vigorously in one spot and tore through to the foam. With the lid closed and more than a smear of paint on the paper it stay wet and usable for several days.

Not much to say about these. They are glue sticks. They melt. They hold stuff together. The box is difficult to get open, but the contents are  bagged in 30 count lots so they are easy to store without getting all over the place.
A fabulous selection of well detailed key and lock charms.  Really interesting designs and well made.  Super fun!

I can't wait to work with these. I wanted them to use for leftover paint from pouring, but they are also going to be good for freehand painting  as well.  Very durable. Use these to make an inexpensive handmade gift to cheer someone up.

These are pretty much like the rest that I have. Easy to clean if you have big puddles or bigger drips. Not so easy if you just have a smear of resin or paint. They need to send along some kind of a scraper tool to help clean these.
I used this to do some paint pouring today. They mixed up well and I got a good result.

I have multiple ideas for using these. They aren't too thick in depth, but will still require a lot of resin to pour completely.  The screw eyes and twine are all included enough to make several sets of these. A darker color pour with some star glitter is going to be fab!
Great paint for canvases or crafts. Mixes well to  use in paint pouring. Very saturated colors.
These are the absolute best for anyone doing steampunk art.  A good selection of gears for sure. Variety of sizes with good detail and well made.
I can't get enough of these charms to use in resin work. So many applications for fun and cheerful items to gift in this difficult time.  They are great in shakers!

I've had bottle cutters for 50 years. I thought it was time to try something a little different.  This is well made with a few caveats. The knobs that hold the adjustable stop for the bottle are not able to be tightened from the top. You have to pick up the whole cutter and tighten the nuts on the underside. It's a royal pain in the butt because then your settings get moved and everything goes wonky. It took me 2 bottles and 4 tries to get a clean break. The rubber rings are hard to position. I think instead of boiling water the next time I will heat the score with a flame and then do the cold water. It took quite a few passes to get it break.

Lovely glitter. Very fine. Good for slime and some resin applications. What I really hate is the packaging. Not even sip bags. You have to cut the little pouches open and then there is no good way to close them. Be prepared for a mess. Hard to dispense from.  Really not the best system.
Excellent product comes with joiners. Very easy to cut to length, Will be using this for for painting.
I was looking for something to use for the center of geode slices, but this is frankly kind of blah and uninteresting.  I may try it, but It really is just flaked mica with no color.

I have been using different kinds of resin for months now. Art n Glow, Amazing, Diamond, etc.  I have measuring pour cups to get the ration right.  I did a small pour to test this and everything I unmolded was flexible. Not sticky just not rigid.  Kind of a shame because it didn't have a lot of bubbles and seemed to have a good working time.  I tried a casting today and measured by weight so we will see if that matters. It worked the same, so I expect another failure when unmolding.
Good canvas boards for artists of all size. Great for painting, collage, or encaustic work.  They do warp with paint pouring.

Who knows. There is no branding on the actual tool. There are no included instructions either.  Do we use glue, do we not? What's what? 3 packs of rhinestones with multiple duplicates and a limited color palette.  I'm glad I didn't have to pay for this.
Looking forward to making some resin key chains and these are just the ticket. They are sturdy and well made.
A little of this goes a long way. I found that in order for it to diffuse you have to use white with whatever color you are using or it really doesn't move in the epoxy.

Most all of the other mica powders I have are in bags. They are hard to seal and messy. These little jars are a much better way to store the powder. I mixes in resin very well and creates some beautiful effects.
WOW. There are a lot of cool designs in this pack. Lots of uses including gelli printing. Nice size for a lot of different projects. Very good.
If you tend to be hard on needles these are good enough. They all break at some point anyway.
Expensive, but good quality.

Maybe I'm spoiled because I have been painting with FineTec metallic watercolors. In any event these have practically no sparkle/ shimmer at all. They are fun to use and write with, but when it dries is is pretty flat and unexciting.

A perfect collection of everything you need to do minor mending chores.  Since my sewing machine and thread are in a separate place from my house this is handy for those times I need to sew on a button or fix a tear. Great selection of thread colors to match anything!

I found these pencils to be nothing special at all. The standard colored pencil palette. Nothing new there. They do blend well on smooth paper although the colors are not very saturated. There are no really good reds in this palette and therefore I'm not a big fan.

I found these to be a fairly consistent size and thickness.  Fairly smooth, but may need more sanding depending on what your project is. The hole that is drilled is kind of larger than it needs to be for stringing. Was hoping it might be a bit less noticeable.

I haven't tried to do this yet, but I opened the kit and laid everything out. Read the directions and looked at everything. I have to say that this is one of the best put together and well instructed things like this that I have seen in 20 years. It's great for anyone that wants to try to do carving. I however don't think it is really for beginners. I do have some experience, but carving can be dangerous and something simpler would be better for a first timer.  I can't wait to get started on this!

I wanted these for some inexpensive resin pendants I made for gifts and for festival season. They are perfect for my use. The cording is fairly flexible and they have a nice lay to them when strung with a pendant.
Good sturdy paper that stands up to water. Very smooth on one side and textured on the other. Fun to get a flow going with paint.

First off this has a really obnoxious odor. The minute I opened the roll I thought I would be ill.  Secondly it is very thick which makes it hard to really burnish down around your vinyl. I did get it to work, but be aware you will have to rub really hard to get it to stick everywhere you need it to.
This paper isn't even as thick as cheap copy paper. Not all what is expected of a good marker paper. Of course because of the thin nature of it, the markers bleed straight through.

Since there wasn't a picture or a description I took a chance on this.  What it is is a tape runner like you would use for scrapbooking or making cards.  Very low tack.  A strip wouldn't even hold 2 pieces of paper together. It is easy to remove by rubbing it off of the paper so I imagine there may be uses for it in a very temporary capacity. I've used Tombow runners before, but this tape wasn't nearly as good.

I seem to have a thing for washi tape these days. I do a lot of art mail and this is handy.  Upon opening the packs it looks like a lot of tape at first glance. However when you look closer, the core is three times as thick as the tape that is on it. So...not as good a deal as you first think.
I can't wait to get going on these. Bright colors. Well marked diamond packages.  Sturdy adhesive. These are are going to be gorgeous!

Quite a bit better kit then one I got previously. Still no felting instructions per se, but at least they tell you in this one how to put the needles in the holder. Lots of bits and pieces and nice templates. Large and smaller eyes for critters and nice colors of wool.

These inks are wonderful for many uses in artwork. My latest fascination is resin and this set has some really neat colors and metallics. I deducted a star because of the packaging. Whenever I have to get tools to open something I get unhappy.
I finally was able to unmold this this morning.  Came out without a problem. Beautiful coasters. It takes a lot of resin though to do this.

Not a fan of the way these are bottled. They are sealed so it requires a small pin to open each bottle. The contents are apparently under pressure because as soon as the seal is broken they spray out all over you. My hands look like I've been playing in Easter Egg Dye. The colors are exceedingly vibrant to the point of blinding. One drop is the most you would need for coloring anything, but because of the delivery system you are going to get way more. I made a resin bracelet and used less then a drop of the turquoise for the whole thing so go way light on use.

I have been reviewing a lot of art supplies lately. They seem to go from really good to not great. This falls into the later category. Not a very good product. Nicely packaged. Too hard are something they really don't work on dry watercolor paper or on wet. Just weird.
Standard selection of diameters for this sort of thing. Not happy because they smear with water based media. Seem to be OK with alcohol markers, but anything water based and they got a little smeary.

The split rings in this set are very sturdy. The hooks are pretty good, but I don't think they are going to hold up to a lot of strain. Some are little wonky as factory stuff is. They are absolutely fine enough though for craft projects for kids or for bazaar selling.

All of these markers seem to be from the same company, but with different names. They have a good brush tip and a fairly fine fine tip.  Standard color palette and somewhat blendable on the right paper.

These are very high quality seals. I love that they are interchangeable. If you are using the little wax octagons that you melt in a spoon, be prepared to use at least 3 of them to get a good impression. These are larger than they look and it takes a lot of wax to do it right. The downside of these is as soon as you lay them down they roll. Either off the table or out of reach. It would be nice if they had just a small flat spot on the handle or some other means to keep them from rolling.
They fit and feed very well in my High temp mini gun. Not much else to say. They work!
Nice beginner simple set of paint pens. Basic colors. Shake, press and paint. Medium nibs.
Been reviewing a lot of paint pens this week. They are all pretty much the same. Rich colors in these. The nib is a bit big to write with if you plan to use these on rocks.

These are fine stir sticks. These come in handy for lots of projects and it's nice to have a big bag to use from.  I also use these to make plant sticks. Use little wooden cutouts and glue them to the top of the stick. Paint and decorate and Voila!

I am very thrilled with the color palette of this thread.  It is a wonderful assortment for making friendship bracelets or doing embroidery. The floss is as good a quality as any other brand name I have used.

I do a lot of mail art. Washi tape is fun for decoration envelopes when you mail and sealing the flaps against mail damage. This sticks well. I deducted one star because the "random" selection didn't really include anything that was in the picture. Got lots of rolls with French writing and drab colors.

Just looking in the bag is fun. So many fun ideas come to mind when you see these. They are good quality. I can imagine all kinds of poly clay dragon things with these. Lots of colors and variations as well. Good product.

I love watercolor canvases for all kinds of painting. Obviously watercolor, Inktense and watercolor pencil are great. I also like them for acrylic painting because you get a smoother finish and it is easier to move the paint around. These are good quality. Grab some because they are hard to find unless you get them online.
I've bought a lot of gemstone beads and these seem to be very nice.  The holes are uniform and even and the beads are consistent in size. These will make gorgeous gemstone trees.
Just finished initiating a return replete with chipboard stuck to the mat that I couldn't remove. Buy the name brand kids. At least you know what you're gonna get.

I have been using Tombow pencils for a long time because I draw a lot of detailed patterns. This set is very nice especially for the hard range from 2 on up. They seem to be fairly durable as well. stay sharp and no broken points. I like the case to keep them all organized. Much better than a box except the lid doesn't stay on too well. First product I've had from this company that I can recommend.

Nothing special about these paints. A good selection as a beginner or travel set, which is what I needed. My other travel palette was getting empty. The colors are not particularly vibrant and like the paint in the tubes there is some sort of odd smell. Not as bad as the tubes by a long stretch, but I get a whiff of it now and then.  Pretty much dollar store paint. Nice tin for storing.

I got this hoping for a good result on glass and paper. So far I've tried it on glass and it seems to be really bonded on there which I wasn't expecting. I tried the glitter variety first and I'm not in love with that so much. Not very glittery at all. I have an issue with the stencils and the lack of instruction there of. They are sticky vinyl stencils and it would be nice to  know if they can be reused and what the cleaning protocols are to do that. Lots of colors. Not as puffy or 3 D as some other paint of this nature, but I think there are good possibilities for a glass project I have in mind.
This does have a long working time as stated. It does not release the air bubbles easily as also stated. You still have to use a torch or a heat gun.
I make gemstone trees and this wire seems to be working well. Not a lot on the spool though.

I love Liquitex products. They are affordable, yet high quality. In my art career I have used a lot of acrylic paints of various kinds. Keeping a mixing chart for colors is something I'm not good at. I prefer to mix on the fly which isn't a good idea. These paints are perfect for mixing. The bottles have a nice fine tipped top that allows you to count drops if that's what you want. 1 drop of this and 3 drops of that equals whatever. Very easy to place the paint. Not too thick. Great blending and a reasonable dry time. Vibrant and rich. I think I have just found my new liquid acrylic paint.

I was really expecting to not be pleased with these. I am therefore impressed. The brush heads themselves are very good. They keep their shape and they don't shed. The handles on the other hand are cheap plastic an the metal ferrules that hold the heads on are not all straight on the handle. Nice palette knife. The case is great for keeping everything together if you are a student traveling to classes. All in all I would recommend this product.

Really impressed with these paints. They are smooth and moist with a great color saturation. Easy to blend on paper or in a palette. Good selection of basic colors that allow a wide variety of mixing for other shades. Like these much better than the Soho paints I have been using.

This was my first diamond project. The kit was well made, with plenty of diamonds. Shipped fast. Just be aware that it isn't going to look anything like the picture you see here. The colors are not anywhere close. Yellow eyes would have been nice on an owl, but they are really a kind of demonic red color. The background and a lot of the other colors are so subtle as to be hardly different than the other. Could have been done with half the colors they sent. Fun to do for those little moments of time that you have during the day. A bright magnifier light is a must for this project!
Made my first hat in about 2 hours and now I am "hooked"! Nice set of looms for a beginner to this craft.

Use a lot of this stuff in my art studio. If you need a quick drying glue for paper work, this is it. Doesn't wrinkle or warp the paper. Dries very fast. Been using it for years and I won't use anything else.
Love this size plate. Great for stamping. Have been able to make some really lovely papers with the stamping technique as opposed to straight monoprinting.

As were a lot of people I know, I was really geeked up to try this product. I do mail art and have a huge collection of envelopes, postcards and other art that I store in binder pages in notebooks. Most of us used to separate them on the page by sewing on the machine which is a real pain. Then we hear about this tool and figured to end our woes. Albeit it is better than the sewing machine, but it is overpriced for what it is.

This tool is a glorified soldering iron with a rolling tip. The tip takes a long time to heat up and it has to be just the right temperature or it doesn't work. If you roll it too fast it won't seal, too slow and you melt your plastic. It is hard to use with the metal guide and melts the plastic if you don't use the guide. I was using a versatool that I have with a pointed tip just like the one that comes with this. I used a soldering iron controller and set the temperature and had about the same results. So if you have a versatool, try it before you spend your money on this.

After trying at length yesterday to carve this, I have to say that I didn't think it was as easy to use as the other reviewer stated. I have brand new tools and good hand strength and I found it a bit difficult. I also think it is near impossible to get this unrolled and flattened out to use. I've tried all manner of weighting and warming and as soon as you disengage those devices, it just rolls back up. I think in the future I will just buy the pre cut sheets.
Got this for doing monoprinting on a Gelli plate. It works well, cleans up well and is very sturdy. A must for any printer.
These replacement cutting mats seem to be made differently than the one that came with my Eclips. The adhesion is better and longer lasting.
This blending solution is a necessary item if you are going to use the Ranger Alcohol Inks. You will need this to clean up your work space and your brushes and pads. A larger bottle would be nice.
Price for the amount you get .
Tons of colors,  molds and shapes well, bakes well.
I used this for masks making and it worlds great.
The only difference on this adhesive is that it takes about 6 minutes with the light to completely adhere and dry. Over all is super strong. But doesn’t dry fast as other brands I’ve used.
They are not the quality of Speed Ball but for what I paid they are worth it.  Pay more get more.  Screens are only removable if you take out staples.  Over all I’m satisfied.
Good as expected
Grand daughter loves it

This was my daughters first clay kit and she absolutely loves it! We actually just ordered our second one of these! I would definitely recommend! My daughter is 11 and the only help she needed was baking them in the oven!

The picture shows sculpting tools
It doesn’t come with those. Had to order those separate. If you are in a hurry like me and didn’t notice I just want to make you aware.
Nice size good quality
This set is super tiny. Pictures made it look bigger. Entire kit is the size of a kids chapter book.
Love this set! Great for arts and crafts. We used it to make rainbow glitter slime!

This bobbin thread is very brittle. It doesn’t work well for embroidery with my Innovis 1250. It would cause the thread to break or shred. When I used a different brand of bobbin thread, my sitches came out beautifully. Great value with time saving, but perhaps would work better as just sewing thread.
Clothes were made of cheap material and most of the clothes have broken since my girls started using them. It was a fun set with lots of different clothes though.
Super thin and see-through. Rips easy.
all the colors and extra stuff that comes with it is nice
I love love love this product, it is so easy to use and a ton of fun to use

This fabric is beautifully soft and thick.  The print is adorable!  I'm very happy with it.
Super cute, tiny bells.  Love the noise they make.
My favorite stitch markers.  Easy to use and haven't snapped in half like other markers I've tried.

I usually sew with Brother machines but bought this one on a great deal.  It took some time to get used to, but it's simple to use, including to thread and wind bobbins. I love the digital stitch menu.  I haven't used it very much, but loved it all the times I have.

I get a kick out of using this hook.  My kids beg to play with it; they think it's the greatest toy ever.  I have yet to use it in the dark but plan to just for fun one day.  My only complaint is that the light is so extremely bright it hurts my eyes!  It's not necessarily a bad thing, but it's hard to crochet with spots floating in my eyes!  I still really like this hook.  Besides the light, the grip is nice, I like the wide handle, and the hook part is smooth and doesn't catch in the yarn, which is a problem I've had before with other plastic hooks.  I'd recommend it!

This set of 5 multi well paint pallets are great for any artist who needs a surface to mix their paints.

I have used many of these over the years. Typically these are a bit larger than the ones in this set but these are a great size for smaller projects and wash well.

Great set.

I am a multimedia sculptor and have worked with Mica powders for many years.

These are typically most often used to tint epoxies, makeup and soap but can be used for far more.

I often add a bit of mica to my top clear coat when I want to add sparkle or if I’m looking to add another layer of dimension. These metallic colors can be added to paints to give an iridescent effect or dusted on top of a wet coat to create a variety of finishes.

These colors are vibrant and catch light wonderfully well. The powder is very fine which makes it easy to mix with any medium you wish without it clumping.

This is an outstanding set whether you’ve worked with Micas for years or this is your first foray. This is a set I highly recommend.

I am a multimedia sculptor and have worked with mica powders for many years.

Micas are typically most often used to tint epoxies, makeup and soap but can be, and are, used for far more.

I add a bit of mica prior to my top clear coat when I want to add sparkle or if I’m looking to add another layer of dimension.

The colors in this set are vibrant and catch light wonderfully well. The powder is very fine which makes it easy to mix with any medium you wish without it clumping. As with most chameleon micas the color changing properties depend greatly on the surface to which they are applied, the medium in which they are mixed and the light reflecting.

I’m so often asked about techniques when people have the opportunity to meet me at events. Unlike many artists, I do not believe in keeping trade secrets. I love working with and teaching other artists whenever I have the opportunity. Here are a few quick tips about working with micas.

* First, it’s important to understand micas can pose health dangers due to how fine the particulates are - when working with them, WEAR A MASK. If in, which is incredibly easy to do, they will remain in your lungs indefinitely causing permanent damage to alveoli. Again - WEAR A MASK.
* Micas can be utilized in so many ways - from mixing directly into mediums such as polymer clays or plasters, painting on glass, making acrylic paint, creating makeup or bath bombs, within resin or candle wax - honestly there is very few limits on what micas can be used to add color and sparkle to.
* What makes mica unique to other types of pigment - it will not dilute. Due the mica being stone crushed to a fine powder it does not dilute as other pigments do.

Here are a couple of my tried and tested ways I work with micas regularly

* For acrylic painting - add the approximate amount of mica you want to a small cup (you can add more later if you need to). Make sure it is free of clumps and it helps to spread it over the bottom of the cup by doing a little jiggle. Now add 2 parts acrylic gloss medium and 1 part gloss varnish or water based polyethylene. As with everything I don’t measure, but instead just eyeball it. Once ingredients are combined, mix well paying careful attention to blend any clumps there might be - now add more mica if desired.
* For adding a hint of sparkle - who doesn’t like a flare of the dramatic? In a small spray bottle combine alcohol (for this 70% rubbing alcohol is fine) with a teaspoon of mica. Spray the piece - allow to dry - clear coat.
* As a rub on furniture glaze - add the approximate amount of mica you want to a small cup (you can add more later if you need to). Make sure it is free of clumps and it helps to spread it over the bottom of the cup by doing a little jiggle. Now add clear furniture wax, I’ve been using Dixie Belle Paint Company Best Dang Furniture Wax (10oz, Clear) and love it ( https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07DTJH62P/ref=cm_sw_em_r_mt_dp_D3SHKZFVPYT29NSY49MQ ). Apply as you would any furniture wax.

This is an outstanding chameleon mica set whether you’ve worked with micas for years or this is your first foray. This is a set I highly recommend.
